title: docs: Add comprehensive SSO configuration guide
body:
## Summary

Adds a unified SSO documentation page at `docs/en/enterprise/features/sso.mdx` covering all supported identity providers for both SaaS and Factory deployments.

## What's Included

- **Provider comparison table** — WorkOS, Entra ID, Okta, Auth0, Keycloak with SaaS/Factory availability
- **SaaS SSO** — default WorkOS auth + enterprise custom SSO
- **Factory setup guides** — step-by-step for all 5 providers with exact env vars, redirect URIs, and IdP configuration
- **CLI authentication** — Device Authorization Grant flow, `crewai enterprise configure`, manual config
- **RBAC overview** — permission model, per-resource scoping, custom roles
- **Troubleshooting** — 6 common issues with fixes
- **Environment variables reference** — quick-reference tables for every provider

## Navigation

Added `en/enterprise/features/sso` in the **Manage** group alongside RBAC in docs.json.

## Sources

- Factory docs (replicated-config: entra-id.mdx, okta-sso.mdx, keycloak-sso.mdx)
- CrewAI CLI auth provider source code (`lib/crewai/src/crewai/cli/authentication/providers/`)
- Technical FAQ (authentication.yaml)
- SSO onboarding meeting transcripts

Requested by @mattbroom and @joaomdmoura in #engineering.
author: iris-clawd

reviews.body:
LGTM

Approved

commits.message:
docs: add comprehensive SSO configuration guide

Add SSO documentation page covering all supported identity providers
for both SaaS (AMP) and Factory deployments.

Includes:
- Provider overview (WorkOS, Entra ID, Okta, Auth0, Keycloak)
- SaaS vs Factory SSO availability
- Step-by-step setup guides per provider with env vars
- CLI authentication via Device Authorization Grant
- RBAC integration overview
- Troubleshooting common SSO issues
- Complete environment variables reference

Placed in the Manage nav group alongside RBAC.
fix: add key icon to SSO docs page
fix: broken links in SSO docs (installation, configuration)

title: refactor(rag): replace urllib with requests in pdf loader
body:
## Summary
Replaces `urllib.request` with the `requests` library in PDFLoader for improved reliability and better error handling. Downloads PDFs to temporary files instead of loading entirely into memory.

## Changes
- Replace `urllib.request.urlopen` with `requests.get`
- Download PDFs to temporary files using `tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile`
- Add support for custom headers via kwargs
- Improve error handling and timeout management
- Add `requests` as a new dependency

## Tier Classification
**Tier 2 — Medium Risk**: Non-architectural refactor with minor dependency addition

## Motivation
The `requests` library provides:
- Better error handling and status code validation (`raise_for_status()`)
- More robust header management
- Industry-standard HTTP client with better maintenance
- Improved security compared to `urllib` (no S310 warning)

## Testing
- [x] Standard CI passes
- [x] PDF downloads from URLs work correctly
- [x] Local PDF file loading unchanged
- [ ] Temporary files properly cleaned up

## Rollback Plan
If issues arise, revert to previous `urllib` implementation. No breaking changes to public API.
author: danielfsbarreto

commits.message: refactor(rag): replace urllib with requests in pdf loader

title: feat: auto-update deployment test repo during release
body:
## Summary
- After PyPI publish completes, automatically updates `crewAIInc/crew_deployment_test`:
  - Bumps `crewai[tools]==X.Y.Z` pin in `pyproject.toml`
  - Regenerates `uv.lock` with `--refresh-package` and retry loop
  - Pushes directly to main
- Handles pre-release versions with `--prerelease=allow`
- Also changed `_trigger_pypi_publish` to always wait (was conditional on `skip_enterprise`)

## Test plan
- [ ] Run a release and verify the deployment test repo gets updated with the new version

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds new automation to clone and push directly to `crewAIInc/crew_deployment_test` during releases and changes the release flow to always wait for the PyPI publish workflow, which could break or block releases if the external repo/lock step fails.
> 
> **Overview**
> After a release is tagged, the release workflow now **always waits** for the PyPI publish GitHub Actions run to complete.
> 
> Once publishing finishes, it automatically updates `crewAIInc/crew_deployment_test` by cloning the repo, bumping the `crewai[tools]==<version>` pin in `pyproject.toml`, regenerating `uv.lock` (with retries and `--prerelease=allow` for pre-releases), then committing and pushing the changes directly to `main`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 9f3cf3bc913d06a602375bd3edc1d199dcf044a6. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body: LGTM — auto-updates deployment test repo with new crewai version pin + lockfile regen. Good use of explicit git add for pyproject.toml + uv.lock (avoids the git add . issue from #5097). Retry loop consistent with enterprise release pattern.

title: feat: improve enterprise release resilience and UX
body:
## Summary
- Add `--skip-to-enterprise` flag to resume just Phase 3 after a partial release failure
- Pass `--prerelease=allow` to `uv sync` for alpha/beta/rc versions so the resolver finds pre-release packages
- Retry `uv sync` up to 10 times (15s intervals) to handle PyPI CDN propagation delay
- Add `update_pyproject_version()` to bump `version = "X.Y.Z"` in `pyproject.toml` (fixes `apps/api` version not updating)
- Print enterprise PR URL in terminal output

## Test plan
- [ ] `devtools release <version> --skip-to-enterprise` runs only Phase 3
- [ ] Pre-release versions resolve correctly with `--prerelease=allow`
- [ ] `uv sync` retries on failure instead of immediately exiting
- [ ] `apps/api/pyproject.toml` version field gets bumped
- [ ] PR URL is printed after enterprise PR creation

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it changes the automated release workflow (enterprise bumping, dependency syncing, and PR creation), which could affect release reliability if the new branching/flag logic or retry behavior is wrong.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves the `devtools release` enterprise phase to be more resumable and robust.
> 
> Adds `update_pyproject_version()` so enterprise `pyproject.toml` files have their `version = "..."` updated (not just dependency pins), and introduces `--skip-to-enterprise` to run only the enterprise release phase with validation against `--skip-enterprise`.
> 
> Makes enterprise `uv sync` more resilient by allowing prerelease resolution (`--prerelease=allow` when releasing alphas/betas/rcs) and retrying `uv sync` up to 10 times to handle PyPI propagation delays, and prints the created enterprise PR URL for easier follow-up.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4a86f7228555f2ff7da286e0c842b68327cd0dee. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body: LGTM — retry loop, prerelease flag, skip-to-enterprise, and pyproject version updater are all solid additions. Minor note: check indentation around the new update_pyproject_version + update_pyproject_dependencies blocks (lines ~1168-1178) — they should both be inside the  guard.

LGTM — retry loop, prerelease flag, skip-to-enterprise, and pyproject version updater are all solid additions. Minor note: check indentation around the new update_pyproject_version + update_pyproject_dependencies blocks — they should both be inside the if pyproject.exists() guard.

commits.message:
feat: improve enterprise release resilience and UX

- Add --skip-to-enterprise flag to resume just Phase 3 after a failure
- Add --prerelease=allow to uv sync for alpha/beta/rc versions
- Retry uv sync up to 10 times to handle PyPI CDN propagation delay
- Update pyproject.toml [project] version field (fixes apps/api version)
- Print PR URL after creating enterprise bump PR

title: fix: bust uv cache for freshly published packages in enterprise release
body:
## Summary
- Adds `--refresh-package crewai --refresh-package crewai-tools` to the `uv sync` call in `_release_enterprise`
- Fixes enterprise release phase failing because uv's cached index doesn't yet include the just-published package version on PyPI

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `uv run devtools release` and verify the enterprise phase `uv sync` succeeds without stale cache issues

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: this only changes the enterprise release automation to force-refresh specific packages during `uv sync`, reducing failures from stale dependency index/cache without impacting runtime code paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Ensures the enterprise release workflow reliably picks up freshly published PyPI versions by changing the `uv sync` step in `_release_enterprise` to pass `--refresh-package` for `crewai`, `crewai-tools`, and `crewai-files` (instead of a plain `uv sync`). This busts `uv`'s cache during the post-publish workspace sync so the just-released packages resolve correctly.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4054ecf5340df5bf45d2cedd7ce4a67506ad22c3. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body: LGTM — necessary cache bust for freshly published packages during enterprise release. Clean and targeted fix.

commits.message:
fix: bust uv cache for freshly published packages in enterprise release

uv sync in the enterprise phase can fail when its cached index hasn't
picked up the just-published crewai/crewai-tools versions on PyPI.
Use --refresh-package to force re-fetching those specific packages.
fix: also refresh crewai-files package in enterprise release sync

title: docs: comprehensive RBAC permissions matrix and deployment guide
body:
## Summary

Addresses user feedback that RBAC documentation is insufficient — members couldn't determine which permissions to configure for common use cases like deploying from GitHub or Zip.

## Changes

### Feature Permissions Matrix
- Full matrix of all 11 feature permissions with Owner and Member defaults
- Description of what each permission controls
- Explanation of Manage / Read / No access levels

### Deployment Guide
- Step-by-step permission requirements for deploying from GitHub
- Step-by-step permission requirements for deploying from Zip
- Quick-reference table with minimum required permissions

### Entity-Level Permissions
- Deployment permission types: `run`, `traces`, `manage_settings`, `human_in_the_loop`, `full_access`
- Entity RBAC for environment variables, LLM connections, and Git repositories
- How entity-level RBAC interacts with feature-level permissions

### Common Role Patterns
- **Developer** — build and deploy, no org settings access
- **Viewer / Stakeholder** — monitor and view results only
- **Ops / Platform Admin** — manage infrastructure without building agents

## Context
User feedback: *"RBAC is too restrictive: members can't build or deploy agents without owner access. No clear documentation on how to configure roles for a developer profile."*

This PR directly addresses the gap by documenting every permission, showing what's needed for common workflows, and providing ready-to-use role templates.
author: iris-clawd

commits.message:
docs: comprehensive RBAC permissions matrix and deployment guide

- Add full feature permissions matrix (11 features × permission levels)
- Document Owner vs Member default permissions
- Add deployment guide: what permissions are needed to deploy from GitHub or Zip
- Document entity-level permissions (deployment permission types: run, traces, manage_settings, HITL, full_access)
- Document entity RBAC for env vars, LLM connections, and Git repositories
- Add common role patterns: Developer, Viewer/Stakeholder, Ops/Platform Admin
- Add quick-reference table for minimum deployment permissions

Addresses user feedback that RBAC was too restrictive and unclear:
members didn't know which permissions to configure for a developer profile.

title: fix: preserve method return value as flow output for @human_feedback with emit
body:
**Bug:** When a `@human_feedback` decorated method with `emit=` is the final method in a flow (its outcome doesn't trigger any downstream listener), the flow's final output is the collapsed outcome string (e.g. `'approved'`) instead of the method's actual return value (e.g. a state dump dict).

**Reproduction:** The [demo_regulatory_risk_scoring](https://github.com/crewAIInc/demo_regulatory_risk_scoring) flow has a `review_report` method that returns `self.custom_state.risk_report.model_dump()` but the flow output shows `'approved'` — the LLM-collapsed outcome from the HITL feedback.

**Root cause:** `_process_feedback()` in `human_feedback.py` returns the collapsed outcome string when `emit` is set. The sync/async wrapper returns this as the method's output. The flow framework uses this same return value for both routing AND as the method's stored output (which becomes the final flow result).

**Fix (two files):**

1. **`human_feedback.py`** — In both sync and async wrappers, stash the real `method_output` on the flow instance (`self._human_feedback_method_output`) before returning the collapsed outcome for routing.

2. **`flow.py`** — In `_execute_method`, after appending the routing result to `_method_outputs`, check for the stashed output. If found, replace the last entry with the real output and clear the stash.

**Backward compatible:**
- Routing still uses the collapsed outcome string (listeners match on `'approved'`, `'needs_revision'`, etc.)
- Only the stored method output changes — from the routing string to the method's actual return value
- Flows without `@human_feedback` or without `emit=` are completely unaffected

**Tests:** 4 new tests covering final output preservation, routing still working, async methods, and method_outputs correctness.

4 files changed, +171 −8
author: alex-clawd

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview Fixes flow final output semantics for terminal `@human_feedback(..., emit=...)` methods so that routing still uses the collapsed outcome string (e.g., `"approved"`), while the flow’s stored/final output preserves the method’s real return value (e.g., a dict/state dump). **Changes:** - Stash the real `@human_feedback` method return value inside the flow instance while still returning the collapsed outcome for routing. - In `Flow._execute_method`, replace the just-appended routing result in `_method_outputs` with the stashed real method output. - Update/add tests to assert final output preservation, routing behavior, async behavior, and `_method_outputs` contents. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/crewai/src/crewai/flow/human_feedback.py | Stashes the wrapped method’s real output when `emit` is used, while continuing to return the collapsed outcome for routing. | | lib/crewai/src/crewai/flow/flow.py | Adds a flow-level stash field and swaps `_method_outputs[-1]` to preserve the real method output for final flow output. | | lib/crewai/tests/test_human_feedback_integration.py | Updates edge-case assertions to expect flow output == method output while outcome remains for routing. | | lib/crewai/tests/test_human_feedback_decorator.py | Adds a new test suite covering terminal output preservation, routing with downstream listener, async, and `_method_outputs`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: preserve method return value as flow output for @human_feedback with emit

When a @human_feedback decorated method with emit= is the final method in a
flow (no downstream listeners triggered), the flow's final output was
incorrectly set to the collapsed outcome string (e.g., 'approved') instead
of the method's actual return value (e.g., a state dict).

Root cause: _process_feedback() returns the collapsed_outcome string when
emit is set, and this string was being stored as the method's result in
_method_outputs.

The fix:
1. In human_feedback.py: After _process_feedback, stash the real method_output
   on the flow instance as _human_feedback_method_output when emit is set.

2. In flow.py: After appending a method result to _method_outputs, check if
   _human_feedback_method_output is set. If so, replace the last entry with
   the stashed real output and clear the stash.

This ensures:
- Routing still works correctly (collapsed outcome used for @listen matching)
- The flow's final result is the actual method return value
- If downstream listeners execute, their results become the final output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
style: ruff format flow.py

fix: use per-method dict stash for concurrency safety and None returns

Addresses review comments:
- Replace single flow-level slot with dict keyed by method name,
  safe under concurrent @human_feedback+emit execution
- Dict key presence (not value) indicates stashed output,
  correctly preserving None return values
- Added test for None return value preservation

title: feat: add enterprise release phase to devtools release
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it extends the release automation to clone and modify a separate enterprise repo and to block on external systems (GitHub Actions + PyPI availability), which can fail in new ways or affect release correctness.
> 
> **Overview**
> `devtools release` is extended into a 3-phase end-to-end workflow that, after tagging and creating the public GitHub release, **triggers the PyPI publish workflow (optionally waits for completion)** and then **clones and releases an enterprise repo** (version bumps, dependency pin update, `uv sync`, PR creation + merge polling, then tag + GitHub release). A new `--skip-enterprise` flag and required enterprise env vars (`ENTERPRISE_REPO`, `ENTERPRISE_VERSION_DIRS`, `ENTERPRISE_CREWAI_DEP_PATH`, optional `ENTERPRISE_EXTRA_PACKAGES`) gate this behavior.
> 
> Refactors/versioning helpers were generalized (shared `_pin_crewai_deps`, `update_pyproject_dependencies` supports extra packages) and PR-merge polling now supports cross-repo PRs; `bump`/`tag` now prompt with a confirmation note clarifying they do not perform publishing or enterprise release.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0010faa10395ca49082b379fb89716ffe2123014. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
## Code Review

**Summary:** This PR extends the devtools release CLI with an enterprise release phase — cloning a configured enterprise repo, bumping versions, updating the `crewai[tools]` pin, running `uv sync`, opening a PR, polling until merged, then tagging and creating a GitHub release. It also adds PyPI publish triggering with optional wait, refactors the dep-pinning regex into a reusable `_pin_crewai_deps` helper, and makes `_poll_pr_until_merged` support cross-repo PRs.

**Overall:** Clean, well-structured extension. The refactoring of shared helpers is good. A few minor observations below — nothing blocking.

### Minor observations

1. **`urlopen` without timeout in `_wait_for_pypi`** — The `urlopen(url)` call on line ~1057 has no `timeout` parameter. If PyPI hangs on a single request, this could block indefinitely (the outer deadline only checks between requests). Consider adding `timeout=10` or similar: `urlopen(url, timeout=10)`.

2. **`git add .` in enterprise release** — After `uv sync`, `git add .` will stage everything in the cloned repo, including lockfile changes and any other artifacts `uv sync` may produce. This is probably intentional (you want the updated lockfile committed), but worth confirming — if `uv sync` ever writes unexpected files, they'd get swept in.

3. **PyPI publish run discovery race** — In `_trigger_pypi_publish(wait=True)`, the 120s polling loop looks for an in-progress workflow run. If the workflow hasn't been queued yet, or if there's already an older in-progress run from a different trigger, this could pick up the wrong run or time out. The window seems reasonable in practice, but a more robust approach would be to capture the run ID from the `gh workflow run` trigger output if possible.

4. **Enterprise tag format** — The enterprise release uses `tag_name = version` (e.g., `1.10.3`), while the main repo appears to use a `v`-prefixed tag for stable releases. If this is intentional (different convention for enterprise), no i

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
Re-approving. New commits address review feedback, extract shared dep pinning logic, and add ENTERPRISE_EXTRA_PACKAGES env var for flexible dep pinning.
Looks good — clean refactoring, well-structured 3-phase flow. Minor hardening suggestions (urlopen timeout, git add -u, workflow race window) can be addressed in a follow-up.

title: Fix: bad credentials for traces batch push (404)
body:
## Summary ### Core fixes
Fix silent 404 cascade on trace event send When `_initialize_backend_batch` failed, `trace_batch_id` was left populated with a client-generated UUID never registered server-side. All subsequent event sends hit a non-existent batch endpoint and returned 404. Now all three failure paths (None response, non-2xx status, exception) clear `trace_batch_id`.
Fix first-time deferred batch init silently skipped First-time users have `is_tracing_enabled_in_context() = False` by design. This caused `_initialize_backend_batch` to return early without creating the batch, and `finalize_batch` to skip finalization (same guard). The first-time handler now passes `skip_context_check=True` to bypass both guards, calls `_finalize_backend_batch` directly, gates `backend_initialized` on actual success, checks `_send_events_to_backend` return status (marking batch as failed on 500), captures event count/duration/batch ID before they're consumed by send/finalize, and cleans up all singleton state via `_reset_batch_state()` on every exit path.
Sync is_current_batch_ephemeral on batch creation success When the batch is successfully created on the server, `is_current_batch_ephemeral` is now synced with the actual `use_ephemeral` value used. This prevents endpoint mismatches where the batch was created on one endpoint but events and finalization were sent to a different one, resulting in 404.
Route mark_trace_batch_as_failed to correct endpoint for ephemeral batches `mark_trace_batch_as_failed` always routed to the non-ephemeral endpoint (`/tracing/batches/{id}`), causing 404s when called on ephemeral batches — the same class of endpoint mismatch this PR aims to fix. Added `mark_ephemeral_trace_batch_as_failed` to `PlusAPI` and a `_mark_batch_as_failed` helper on `TraceBatchManager` that routes based on `is_current_batch_ephemeral`.
Gate backend_initialized on actual init success (non-first-time path) On the non-first-time path, `backend_initialized` was set to `True` unconditionally after `_initialize_backend_batch` returned. With the new failure-path cleanup that clears `trace_batch_id`, this created an inconsistent state: `backend_initialized=True` + `trace_batch_id=None`. Now set via `self.trace_batch_id is not None`.
### Resilience improvements
Retry transient failures on batch creation `_initialize_backend_batch` now retries up to 2 times with 200ms backoff on transient failures (None response, 5xx, network errors). Non-transient 4xx errors are not retried. The short backoff minimizes lock hold time on the non-first-time path where `_batch_ready_cv` is held.
Fall back to ephemeral on server auth rejection When the non-ephemeral endpoint returns 401/403 (expired token, revoked credentials, key rotation), the client automatically switches to ephemeral tracing instead of losing traces. The fallback forwards `skip_context_check` and is guarded against infinite recursion — if ephemeral also fails, `trace_batch_id` is cleared normally.
Fix action-event race initializing batch as non-ephemeral `_handle_action_event` called `batch_manager.initialize_batch()` directly, defaulting `use_ephemeral=False`. When a `DefaultEnvEvent` or `LLMCallStartedEvent` fired before `CrewKickoffStartedEvent` in the thread pool, the batch was locked in as non-ephemeral. Now routes through `_initialize_batch()` which computes `use_ephemeral` from `_check_authenticated()`.
Guard _mark_batch_as_failed against cascading network errors When `_finalize_backend_batch` failed with a network error (e.g. `[Errno 54] Connection reset by peer`), the exception handler called `_mark_batch_as_failed` — which also makes an HTTP request on the same dead connection. That second failure was unhandled. Now wrapped in a try/except so it logs at debug level instead of propagating.
Design decision: first-time users always use ephemeral First-time trace collection **always creates ephemeral batches**, regardless of authentication status. This is intentional: 1. **The first-time handler UX is built around ephemeral traces** — it displays an access code, a 24-hour expiry link, and opens the browser to the ephemeral trace viewer. Non-ephemeral batches don't produce these artifacts, so the handler would fall through to the "Local Traces Collected" fallback even when traces were successfully sent. 2. **The server handles account linking automatically** — `LinkEphemeralTracesJob` runs on user signup and migrates ephemeral traces to permanent records. Logged-in users can access their traces via their dashboard regardless. 3. **Checking auth during batch setup broke event collection** — moving `_check_authenticated()` into `_initialize_batch` caused the batch initialization to fail silently during the flow/crew start event handler, preventing all event collection. Keeping the first-time path fast and side-effect-free preserves event collection. The auth check is deferred to the non-first-time path (second run onwards), where `is_tracing_enabled_in_context()` is `True` and the normal tracing pipeline handles everything — including the 401/403 ephemeral fallback.
### Manual tests
Matrix | Scenario | First run | Second run | |----------|-----------|------------| | Logged out, fresh `.crewai_user.json` | Ephemeral trace created, URL returned | Ephemeral trace created, URL returned | | Logged in, fresh `.crewai_user.json` | Ephemeral trace created, URL returned | Trace batch finalized, URL returned | | Flow execution | Tested with `poem_flow` | Tested with `poem_flow` | | Crew execution | Tested with `hitl_crew` | Tested with `hitl_crew` |
--- > [!NOTE] > **Medium Risk** > Touches trace upload/finalization paths and adds retry/fallback logic around network/auth failures, which could affect telemetry delivery and error handling in production. Changes are contained to tracing and include expanded tests to reduce regression risk. > > **Overview** > Fixes tracing batch endpoint mismatches that could cascade into 404s by clearing `trace_batch_id` on backend-init failures, syncing `is_current_batch_ephemeral` on successful init, and routing “mark failed” calls to the correct ephemeral vs non-ephemeral endpoint. > > Improves resilience and first-run UX by adding a small retry/backoff to batch initialization, falling back to ephemeral tracing on 401/403, guarding first-time deferred uploads with `skip_context_check`, and resetting singleton batch state after completion/failure. Also reduces init race issues by ensuring flow/crew ownership is claimed deterministically and action events initialize batches via `_initialize_batch` (respecting ephemeral/auth decisions). > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e0358c05b2296fe3ebf658cc76c769b2f2bfd782. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
author: akaKuruma

reviews.body: Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).

commits.message:
fix: clear trace_batch_id on backend batch initialization failure

  When _initialize_backend_batch fails (None response, non-2xx status,
  or exception), trace_batch_id was left populated with a client-generated
  UUID that was never registered server-side. Subsequent calls to
  _send_events_to_backend would see the stale ID and POST events to
  /ephemeral/batches/{id}/events, resulting in a 404 from the server.

  Nullify trace_batch_id on all three failure paths so downstream methods
  skip event sending instead of hitting a non-existent batch.

fix: gate backend_initialized on actual batch creation success

  In first_time_trace_handler._initialize_backend_and_send_events,
  backend_initialized was set to True unconditionally after calling
  _initialize_backend_batch, regardless of whether the server-side
  batch was actually created. This caused _send_events_to_backend and
  finalize_batch to run against a non-existent batch.

  Now check trace_batch_id after _initialize_backend_batch returns;
  if None (batch creation failed), call _gracefully_fail and return
  early, skipping event send and finalization.

feat: add retry logic for ephemeral trace batch creation

  Transient failures (None response, 5xx, network errors) during
  _initialize_backend_batch now retry up to 2 times with a 1s backoff.
  Non-transient 4xx errors (422 validation, 401 auth) are not retried
  since the same payload would fail again. If all retries are exhausted,
  trace_batch_id is cleared per the existing safety net.

  This runs post-execution when the user has already answered "y" to
  view traces, so the ~2s worst-case delay is acceptable.

fix: respect authentication status for first-time users

  Previously, _initialize_batch forced use_ephemeral=True for all
  first-time users, bypassing _check_authenticated() entirely. This
  meant logged-in users in a new project directory were routed to the
  ephemeral endpoint instead of their account's tracing endpoint.

  Now _check_authenticated() runs for all users including first-time.
  Authenticated first-time users get non-ephemeral tracing (traces
  linked to their account); only unauthenticated first-time users
  fall back to ephemeral. The deferred backend init in
  FirstTimeTraceHandler also reads is_current_batch_ephemeral instead
  of hardcoding use_ephemeral=True.

feat: fall back to ephemeral tracing on server auth rejection

  When the non-ephemeral batch endpoint returns 401 or 403 (expired
  token, revoked credentials, JWKS rotation), _initialize_backend_batch
  now switches is_current_batch_ephemeral to True and retries via the
  ephemeral endpoint. This preserves traces that would otherwise be
  lost due to the timing gap between client-side token validation and
  server-side JWT decode.

  The fallback only triggers on the non-ephemeral path to prevent
  infinite recursion. If the ephemeral attempt also fails, trace_batch_id
  is cleared normally.

  Addresses the 2M+ failed push attempts in which valid client-side
  tokens were rejected on the server.

fix: bypass is_tracing_enabled_in_context for first-time deferred batch init

  First-time users have is_tracing_enabled_in_context() = False by design
  (it's a prerequisite for should_auto_collect_first_time_traces). This
  caused _initialize_backend_batch to return early without creating the
  batch, and _send_events_to_backend to send to a non-existent batch.

  Add skip_context_check parameter to _initialize_backend_batch so the
  first-time handler can bypass the guard during deferred init. Gate
  backend_initialized on trace_batch_id being set. Call
  _finalize_backend_batch directly instead of finalize_batch (which has
  the same context guard). Sync is_current_batch_ephemeral on success
  to prevent endpoint mismatch between batch creation and event send.

fix: always use ephemeral tracing for first-time users

  The first-time handler UX is built around ephemeral traces (access
  code, 24hr expiry link, browser open). Checking auth and creating
  non-ephemeral batches caused the handler to fall through to the
  local traces fallback since ephemeral_trace_url is only set for
  ephemeral batches. The server's LinkEphemeralTracesJob links
  ephemeral traces to user accounts on signup regardless.

  Remove auth check from first-time handler and always pass
  use_ephemeral=True to _initialize_backend_batch.

fix: mark batch as failed when event send fails in first-time handler

  The return value of _send_events_to_backend() was discarded in
  _initialize_backend_and_send_events, so _finalize_backend_batch was
  called unconditionally with the full event count even when the send
  returned 500. This finalized the batch as "completed" on the server
  while it received 0 events, producing an empty trace URL.

  Now check the return status and call mark_trace_batch_as_failed on
  500, matching the behavior of the regular finalize_batch path.

refactor: remove redundant tests in TestTraceBatchIdClearedOnFailure

  Remove test_trace_batch_id_cleared_on_none_response (covered by
  TestInitializeBackendBatchRetry::test_exhausts_retries_then_clears_batch_id)
  and test_trace_batch_id_cleared_on_non_2xx_response (covered by
  TestInitializeBackendBatchRetry::test_no_retry_on_4xx).

fix: address PR review findings — forward skip_context_check in
  recursive fallback, reduce retry backoff, clean up batch state

  - Forward skip_context_check parameter in the 401/403 ephemeral
    fallback recursive call to prevent silent early return when
    is_tracing_enabled_in_context() is False
  - Reduce retry backoff from 1s to 200ms to minimize lock hold time
    on the non-first-time path (worst case 400ms vs 2s)
  - Add batch state cleanup after _finalize_backend_batch in the
    first-time handler, mirroring finalize_batch: reset current_batch,
    event_buffer, trace_batch_id, is_current_batch_ephemeral,
    batch_owner_type, batch_owner_id, and call _cleanup_batch_data()

fix: prevent zeroed data in local trace message and ensure cleanup on all paths

  - Read execution start time non-destructively before _finalize_backend_batch
    consumes it, so the server receives the real duration and the local
    fallback message also shows the correct value
  - Pass pre-captured events_count, duration_ms, and batch_id to
    _show_local_trace_message instead of reading from batch_manager
    (buffer cleared by send, duration consumed by finalize)
  - Extract _reset_batch_state to reset all singleton state (current_batch,
    event_buffer, trace_batch_id, is_current_batch_ephemeral,
    backend_initialized, batch_owner_type/id) and call it in every exit
    path: success, init failure, send failure, and exception handler

fix: resolve first-time trace prompt not appearing for Flows and standalone Crews

  Two places had the same race condition between FlowStartedEvent /
  CrewKickoffStartedEvent handlers and DefaultEnvEvent in the thread pool.

  Commit 929d756ae introduced env-detection events (DefaultEnvEvent etc.)
  dispatched through _handle_action_event, which has a fallback that calls
  initialize_batch() without claiming batch ownership. get_env_context() is
  called at the very top of both Flow.kickoff() and Crew.kickoff(), so
  DefaultEnvEvent can fire and win the thread pool race before the context
  event handler runs. When that happened, is_batch_initialized() returned
  True and _initialize_flow_batch / _initialize_crew_batch were skipped,
  leaving batch_owner_type=None. The completion checks
  (batch_owner_type == "flow" / "crew") then failed silently and the
  first-time trace prompt never appeared.

  Fix: remove the is_batch_initialized() guard from on_flow_started and
  replace it with an unconditional call to _initialize_flow_batch.
  initialize_batch() is already idempotent (lock-guarded early exit), so
  batch_owner_type="flow" is set regardless of which event initialized the
  batch first.

  For on_crew_started, apply the same pattern but guard against overriding
  a parent flow's ownership: call _initialize_crew_batch unconditionally
  unless batch_owner_type is already "flow".

  Also suppress the "Tracing is disabled" panel in ConsoleFormatter when
  the first-time handler is active, preventing a confusing mid-flow message
  before the trace prompt appears at the end.

fix(tracing): reduce retry attempts and skip retries on network exceptions

  `_initialize_backend_batch` executes while holding `_batch_ready_cv`,
  blocking any concurrent thread waiting for batch readiness (e.g. event
  handlers). The previous retry logic could hold the lock for an
  unacceptable amount of time:

    Previous worst case (max_retries=2, all attempts timing out):
      3 attempts × 30s HTTP timeout + 2 × 0.2s sleep = ~90.4s
      Plus auth-fallback recursive call: up to ~180s total

    New worst case (max_retries=1, all attempts timing out):
      2 attempts × 30s HTTP timeout + 1 × 0.2s sleep = ~60.2s

    Exceptions (timeout, connection error) now abort immediately:
      1 attempt × 30s HTTP timeout = ~30s (no sleep, no retry)

  Changes:
  - Reduce max_retries from 2 to 1 (5xx responses still retry once)
  - Move try/except outside the retry loop so any exception
    (timeout, connection error) aborts immediately without retrying
    or sleeping, capping the exception path at a single HTTP timeout

  Addresses review comment on PR #4947 about retry sleeps holding the
  batch initialization lock and adding unexpected latency.

fix(tracing): route mark-as-failed to correct endpoint and gate backend_initialized on success

  mark_trace_batch_as_failed always hit the non-ephemeral endpoint, causing
  404s when called on ephemeral batches — the same class of endpoint mismatch
  this branch aims to fix. Add mark_ephemeral_trace_batch_as_failed to PlusAPI
  and a _mark_batch_as_failed helper on TraceBatchManager that routes based on
  is_current_batch_ephemeral.

  Also gate backend_initialized on trace_batch_id being set so the non-first-time
  path no longer reports success when _initialize_backend_batch actually failed.

fix(tracing): route action-event batch init through _initialize_batch to respect use_ephemeral

  _handle_action_event called batch_manager.initialize_batch() directly,
  defaulting use_ephemeral=False. When a DefaultEnvEvent fired before
  CrewKickoffStartedEvent in the thread pool, the batch was locked in as
  non-ephemeral, causing VCR cassette path mismatches on CI. Route through
  _initialize_batch() which computes use_ephemeral from _check_authenticated().
Fix: ruff format
Fix: mypy errors
Merge branch 'main' into fix/bad-credentials-for-batch-push

title: fix: resolve multiple bugs in HITL flow system
body:
## Summary
- Fix `_deserialize_llm_from_context` mutating input dict via `.pop()`
- Set `_pending_feedback_context` on re-pause in `resume_async` so `flow.pending_feedback` stays consistent
- Use `_method_outputs[-1]` as final result in `resume_async` instead of stale feedback result
- Handle async providers returning coroutines in both sync and async flow methods
- Guarantee a valid routing outcome when `emit` is set in `_process_feedback`
- Defer marking paused method as completed from `from_pending` to `resume_async`
- Fix partial match in `_collapse_to_outcome` preferring longest match to avoid substring false positives
- Safely serialize non-JSON-serializable `method_output` in `PendingFeedbackContext.to_dict()`
- Remove trailing `\n` from `ConsoleProvider.request_input` prompt

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes adjust HITL pause/resume control flow, routing outcomes, and provider invocation semantics, which could affect how flows resume and which listeners run. Risk is moderated but touches core flow execution paths and persistence serialization.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes multiple Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) flow edge cases around pausing/resuming and outcome routing.
> 
> `PendingFeedbackContext.to_dict()` now JSON-safely serializes `method_output`/`metadata`, `_deserialize_llm_from_context()` no longer mutates input dicts, and `ConsoleProvider.request_input()` removes an extra newline in the prompt.
> 
> Flow resumption logic is tightened: completed-method marking is deferred to `resume_async`, re-pauses during resume keep `flow.pending_feedback` consistent, `emit` routing now guarantees a valid outcome (defaulting to `default_outcome`/first option), final flow result prefers the latest `method_outputs[-1]`, and LLM outcome collapsing fallback partial matching now selects the *longest* match to avoid substring false positives. Async feedback providers are supported by awaiting coroutines in async methods and raising a clear error if a coroutine is returned in sync methods.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 71936dbf3b499df83ef4d31e1ab60553be039567. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
Thorough set of HITL flow bugfixes. Reviewed the full diff — approving.

**Key fixes, all correct:**

1. **`_deserialize_llm_from_context` mutation** — `.pop()` was mutating the input dict. Fixed by copying first. Clean.

2. **Deferred completion marking** — Moving `_completed_methods.add()` from `from_pending()` to `resume_async()` is the right call. Methods should only be marked complete after resume processing, not on deserialization. This fixes loop re-execution (e.g., implement_changes → suggest_changes → implement_changes).

3. **`final_result` using `_method_outputs[-1]`** — Previously used the stale feedback result. Now correctly uses the last method output after routing/listener execution. Important for flows where listeners append additional outputs.

4. **Re-pause context preservation** — Setting `_pending_feedback_context` on `HumanFeedbackPending` during resume ensures `flow.pending_feedback` stays consistent across re-pauses.

5. **Async provider support** — Split into `_request_feedback` (sync, rejects coroutines with clear error) and `_request_feedback_async` (awaits coroutines). Good separation.

6. **`_collapse_to_outcome` longest match** — Fixes substring false positives (e.g., "approve" matching before "approve_with_changes"). Longest-wins is the correct strategy.

7. **Guaranteed routing outcome** — When `emit` is set but `collapsed_outcome` is None, falls back to `default_outcome` or first emit option. Prevents None routing.

8. **`_make_json_safe`** — Progressive fallback (primitives → Pydantic → dataclass → str) for serializing method_output/metadata. Handles edge cases well.

9. **Console prompt `\n` removal** — Minor UX fix.

No concerns. Ship it.

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
Re-approving. New commits: recursive dataclass field processing in _make_json_safe + collapsed_outcome fallback in resume_async. Good hardening.

fix: recursively process dataclass fields in _make_json_safe
fix: add collapsed_outcome fallback in resume_async

title: feat: add Qdrant Edge storage backend for memory system
body:
## Summary

- Add `QdrantEdgeStorage` as a pluggable `StorageBackend` implementation backed by Qdrant Edge (`qdrant-edge-py`)
- Uses a write-local/sync-central pattern for safe multi-process access — each worker writes to a PID-keyed local shard, reads merge both shards, and local records flush to central on close
- Support `storage="qdrant-edge"` string in `Memory` for easy opt-in
- Add `qdrant-edge-py` as an optional dependency under `[qdrant-edge]`
- 19 tests covering CRUD, filtering, dual-shard search, sync lifecycle, orphaned shard cleanup, and Memory integration

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces a new persistence backend and changes `Memory.close()` behavior to invoke backend `close()`, which can affect durability and shutdown semantics across processes.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds an optional `QdrantEdgeStorage` backend (via `qdrant-edge-py`) for unified memory, using a **write-local/sync-central** shard layout to support multi-process writes and merged reads, with orphaned worker shard recovery.
> 
> `Memory` now accepts `storage="qdrant-edge"` and calls `storage.close()` on shutdown to flush pending local data; the PR also introduces a new `qdrant-edge` optional dependency and comprehensive tests covering CRUD, filtering, lifecycle flush, dual-shard reads, and orphan cleanup.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit fe4a2ccb7faa6e258043a01b2568771080755bca. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
## Code Review: Qdrant Edge Storage Backend

Well-structured PR with good test coverage (19 tests covering CRUD, filtering, dual-shard behavior, sync lifecycle, orphaned shard cleanup, and Memory integration). The write-local/sync-central pattern is a solid approach for multi-process safety.

### Observations & Suggestions

**1. Cross-process lock not implemented (medium concern)**
The module docstring and class docstring both state that local records flush to central "under a cross-process lock," but no locking mechanism exists in `flush_to_central()` or `close()`. If two worker processes flush simultaneously, upserts to the central shard could race. Consider adding a file lock (e.g., `fcntl.flock` or `filelock` library) around the central shard write path, or update the documentation to clarify the actual concurrency guarantees.

**2. `datetime.utcnow()` is deprecated (minor)**
`datetime.utcnow()` is deprecated as of Python 3.12. Used in `_payload_to_record` (fallback) and `touch_records`. Consider replacing with `datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc)`.

**3. Inefficient `count()` when local shard has data**
When `_local_has_data` is True, `count()` falls back to `len(self.list_records(limit=50_000))`, which scrolls and deserializes all records. Could instead use `CountRequest` on both shards and deduplicate by record_id, or at minimum scroll with `with_payload=False`.

**4. Async methods are synchronous wrappers**
`asave`, `asearch`, and `adelete` call their sync counterparts directly, which will block the event loop. Consider wrapping with `asyncio.to_thread()` — though this may be consistent with other storage backends in the codebase.

**5. PID recycling in orphan cleanup**
`_cleanup_orphaned_shards` uses `os.kill(pid, 0)` to detect dead processes, but PIDs can be recycled by the OS. A long-lived shard from a previous run could match a completely different process. The risk is low given typical PID spaces but worth noting for robustness — a secondary check (e.g.

Re-approving. New commit addresses all review feedback: removed misleading cross-process lock docs, replaced deprecated datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(UTC), improved count() to use scroll+dedup instead of full deserialization, and wrapped async methods with asyncio.to_thread. Clean fixes.

Re-approving after 3 new fixes: delete now intersects record_ids with complex filters, orphaned shard recovery infers vector dimensions to prevent data loss, and cleaned up unused import in tests. Good hardening.

Re-approving. New commits: lazy-import in tests for CI without qdrant-edge, prefer local shard over central when deduplicating search results, and try/finally to guarantee shard close on error. Good robustness improvements.
Re-approving. Latest commit fixes Python 3.10 compat — uses timezone.utc instead of UTC (added in 3.11).

Re-approving. Latest: adds byteorder arg to int.from_bytes() for Python 3.10 compat (keyword-only default added in 3.11).

fix: address review feedback on qdrant edge storage
fix: add logging to orphaned shard ProcessLookupError handler
fix: intersect record_ids with complex filters in delete
fix: infer vector dim from orphaned shard to prevent data loss
fix: remove unused ScopeInfo import from tests
fix: lazy-import QdrantEdgeStorage in tests for CI without qdrant-edge-py
fix: prefer local shard over central when deduplicating search results
fix: add try/finally to guarantee shard close on error

Clean PR. Two changes:

**1. Linter workflow simplification (.github/workflows/linter.yml)**

No behavioral changes, no risk of regression. LGTM.

title: feat: add docs-check command to analyze changes and generate docs with translations
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds a new CLI that can write to the `docs/` tree and relies on OpenAI structured/unstructured outputs; incorrect analysis or path handling could lead to unintended doc changes.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `docs-check` CLI command (wired into `pyproject.toml` scripts and the main `crewai_devtools` CLI) that diffs the current branch against a base ref and uses OpenAI structured output to decide whether docs need to be created/updated.
> 
> With `--write`, it generates or updates the target English MDX file under `docs/en/` and then produces translations for `docs/ar/`, `docs/ko/`, and `docs/pt-BR/`, with a `--dry-run` mode and basic safety checks to prevent writing outside `docs/`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 255b1864bd34cc56299b3dd664692eacffc2455b. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
## Code Review: `docs-check` command

Clean, well-structured addition. The Pydantic models + OpenAI structured output approach is solid, and the CLI ergonomics (`--write`, `--dry-run`) are good. A few observations:

### Suggestions

1. **No error handling for OpenAI / git failures** — If `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset or the API call fails, the user gets a raw traceback. A `try/except` with a friendly `console.print` error in the click command would go a long way.

2. **Full diff passed to `_update_doc`** — For updates, the entire repo diff is sent as `diff_context` (truncated to 10k chars). If the diff is large and the relevant file's changes are near the end, the LLM may never see them. Consider filtering the diff to only the relevant file before passing it in.

3. **Diff truncation is silent** — `diff[:50000]` in `_analyze_diff` drops everything past 50k chars with no warning. Worth logging when truncation occurs so users know large diffs may have incomplete analysis.

4. **No tests** — This is a 457-line new module. Even a few unit tests for `_analyze_diff` (mocking the OpenAI client) and the click command (with `CliRunner`) would catch regressions early.

5. **`load_dotenv()` at module level** — Runs on import even when `docs-check` is not invoked. Moving it inside the `docs_check` function would avoid side effects for other CLI commands.

6. **Dry-run output inconsistency** — `create` shows a 200-char preview of generated content, but `update` just says "Would update" with no preview. Showing a snippet in both cases would make `--dry-run` more useful.

### Minor

- `client.beta.chat.completions.parse` — beta API surface could shift. Not urgent but worth noting for future maintenance.
- No option to target a single translation language — fine for now, but could be useful later.

### Verdict

Looks good for a first iteration of an internal dev tool. The suggestions above are improvements, not blockers. Approving.
Re-approving. All CI green, no new commits since last review. Ship it.
Re-approving after new commits. The fix addresses the empty update content guard and passes diff to doc generation — both improvements from earlier feedback. CI running on latest push.

title: refactor: deduplicate sync/async task execution and kickoff in agent …
body:
…core

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Refactors core agent execution/kickoff flows and retry/error handling, which can subtly change prompting, event emission, and recursion-based retries across sync/async paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Refactors `Agent` task execution to share logic between sync and async flows by extracting helpers for prompt preparation (`_prepare_task_execution`), prompt finalization (`_finalize_task_prompt`), memory retrieval/eventing, and result finalization (`_finalize_task_execution`).
> 
> Standardizes retry/error handling via shared `_check_execution_error` + sync/async handlers, and reuses a single `_build_execution_prompt` for both `create_agent_executor` and standalone kickoff setup. Kickoff paths are also deduplicated with `_finalize_kickoff` and `_emit_kickoff_error`, plus minor correctness/maintainability tweaks (module-level imports, `docker_path` casting, typed `LLMMessage` list in knowledge query, and updated date-injection tests to patch `crewai.agent.core.datetime`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit f27c85fa94beba2935f90a0cf4665657cd080881. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body: Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue. Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).

## Code Review

Clean refactoring that successfully deduplicates the sync/async task execution and kickoff paths. The behavioral semantics are preserved correctly.

### What's Good
- **Well-structured extraction**: `_prepare_task_execution`, `_finalize_task_prompt`, `_retrieve_memory_context`, `_finalize_task_execution`, `_build_execution_prompt`, `_finalize_kickoff`, and `_emit_kickoff_error` are all clean, focused helpers with clear docstrings.
- **Error handling preserved**: The retry recursion pattern in `_handle_execution_error` / `_handle_execution_error_async` maintains the same semantics — `_check_execution_error` correctly uses bare `raise` for passthrough exceptions (works because it's called from within an active except block), and `raise e` for litellm/exhausted-retry cases.
- **Module-level imports**: Moving `datetime`, `json`, `concurrent.futures`, `warnings`, and the event types to the top is the right call. The test patch target update to `crewai.agent.core.datetime` correctly follows from this change.
- **Minor correctness fixes**: `str(docker_path)` cast, typed `LLMMessage` list for knowledge query, `cast(BaseLLM, self.llm)` in the converter, and `@classmethod` on `validate_from_repository` are all good catches.
- **Kickoff dedup**: `_build_execution_prompt` shared between `create_agent_executor` and `_prepare_kickoff` eliminates a significant copy-paste block.

### One Minor Note
`_emit_kickoff_error` uses `raise e` (necessarily, since it's not inside an except block itself), which will produce a slightly different traceback compared to the original bare `raise`. The exception chain is still preserved, so this is a cosmetic difference in stack traces, not a behavioral one. Acceptable tradeoff for the deduplication.

### Verified
- Finalization (`_finalize_task_execution`) runs at each recursion level on retry, matching the original behavior.
- The removed `task=None, crew=None` from the `AgentExecutor` constructor in `_prepare_kickoff` relies on t

title: Fix for agent memory saving
body:
Fix: Add a remember_many() method to the MemoryScope class that delegates to self._memory.remember_many(...) with the scoped path, following the exact same pattern as the existing remember() method.

Problem: When you pass memory=memory.scope("/agent/...") to an Agent, CrewAI's internal code calls remember_many() after every task to persist results. But MemoryScope never implemented remember_many() — only the parent Memory class has it.

Symptom: [ERROR]: Failed to save kickoff result to memory: 'MemoryScope' object has no attribute 'remember_many' — memories are silently never saved after agent tasks.
author: nicoferdi96

commits.message:
Fix for agent memory saving

Fix: Add a remember_many() method to the MemoryScope class that delegates to self._memory.remember_many(...) with the scoped path, following the exact same pattern as the existing remember() method.

Problem: When you pass memory=memory.scope("/agent/...") to an Agent, CrewAI's internal code calls remember_many() after every task to persist results. But MemoryScope never implemented remember_many() — only the parent Memory class has it.

Symptom: [ERROR]: Failed to save kickoff result to memory: 'MemoryScope' object has no attribute 'remember_many' — memories are silently never saved after agent tasks.

title: fix: missing router edges for @listen + @human_feedback(emit=...) methods
body:
## Problem

When a Flow method uses both `@listen` and `@human_feedback(emit=[...])`, the flow serializer fails to generate router edges from that method to the listeners of its emit paths. This causes the Studio Flow Run Tab to show disconnected nodes.

**Example:** The whitepaper generator's `review_infographic` has `@listen(generate_whitepaper)` + `@human_feedback(emit=['approved', 'needs_changes', 'cancelled'])`. The edges to `handle_cancelled`, `send_slack_notification`, and `classify_feedback` were all missing — 6 edges invisible in the UI.

## Root Cause

In `FlowMeta.__init_subclass__`, when a listener method is also a router, the metaclass called `get_possible_return_constants()` to detect router paths via source code analysis. This works for plain `@router` methods (which have explicit return statements), but fails for `@human_feedback(emit=[...])` methods where the paths come from the decorator parameter, stored as `__router_paths__`.

The fix for `@start + @human_feedback` already existed (line 796), but the same fix was missing for `@listen + @human_feedback`.

## Fix

Check `__router_paths__` first (set by `@human_feedback`), fall back to `get_possible_return_constants()` for plain `@router` methods.

## Test

Added test: `@listen` + `@human_feedback(emit=[...])` generates correct router edges — verifies the exact pattern from the whitepaper generator.
author: alex-clawd

commits.message:
fix: use __router_paths__ for listener+router methods in FlowMeta

When a method has both @listen and @human_feedback(emit=[...]),
the FlowMeta metaclass registered it as a router but only used
get_possible_return_constants() to detect paths. This fails for
@human_feedback methods since the paths come from the decorator's
emit param, not from return statements in the source code.

Now checks __router_paths__ first (set by @human_feedback), then
falls back to source code analysis for plain @router methods.

This was causing missing edges in the flow serializer output —
e.g. the whitepaper generator's review_infographic -> handle_cancelled,
send_slack_notification, classify_feedback edges were all missing.

Adds test: @listen + @human_feedback(emit=[...]) generates correct
router edges in serialized output.

Reviewed the full diff — 108 files, 843 additions, 564 deletions. Approving. The vast majority is mechanical type annotation work, but there are some behavioral changes worth calling out:

**Behavioral changes (all look correct):**

1. **BraveSearchToolBase retry exhaustion** — Fixed the fallback when all retries are exhausted. Old code had `last_resp or resp` where `resp` could be undefined. New code properly checks `if last_resp is not None` before calling `_raise_for_error`.

2. **Cookie/env-var handling** (scrape tools) — Changed `os.getenv(cookies['value'])` to `os.getenv(cookies['value']) or ''` in ScrapeElementFromWebsiteTool and ScrapeWebsiteTool. Prevents `None` from being passed as a cookie value.

3. **API key fallbacks** (Patronus, Serper, Zapier) — Added `or ''` fallbacks for `os.getenv()` calls used in HTTP headers. Prevents `None` header values that would cause requests failures.

4. **NL2SQLTool metadata loading** — Added guard for when `_fetch_available_tables()` returns a string (error message) instead of a list. Prevents crash during `model_post_init`.

5. **Selenium scraping tool** — Added runtime guards (`if self.driver is None`) in `_get_body_content`, `_get_elements_content`, and `_make_request`. Also added `close()` null safety.

6. **ContextualAI query tool** — Simplified response extraction from a chain of `hasattr` checks to `getattr(response, 'content', response)`. Cleaner but verify the Contextual AI SDK response shape hasn't changed.

7. **LanceDB adapter** — Changed typed `_db: LanceDBConnection` and `_table: LanceDBTable` to `Any` to avoid import issues. Pragmatic.

8. **Couchbase tool** — Removed fallback assignments (`Cluster = Any`, etc.) in the `except ImportError` block and changed field type to `SkipValidation[Any]`. Note: this changes `tool.specs.json` to now require `cluster` as a required init param — verify this doesn't break toolspec consumers.

Good fix. The `isinstance(mem, Memory)` check from #5048 was too strict — it rejected valid memory implementations that don't subclass `unified_memory.Memory` (like MemoryScope or custom implementations), breaking the learn tests.

`mem is None` is the right guard here since the code is already wrapped in a broad `try/except` — if the memory object doesn't have `recall()` or `remember_many()`, it'll be caught gracefully. The `type: ignore[union-attr]` on `remember_many` is fine given that safety net.

title: fix: pin litellm upper bound to last tested version (1.82.6)
body:
## What
Pin the `litellm` optional dependency upper bound from `<3` to `<=1.82.6`.

## Why
The current specifier `litellm>=1.74.9,<3` allows any future litellm release to be pulled in when customers install `crewai[litellm]`. If litellm ships a breaking change, customers get it immediately with no protection.

By pinning to `<=1.82.6` (current latest on PyPI, known-good), we ensure customers only get litellm versions that have been tested with crewAI. When we want to support newer versions, we bump this bound explicitly after validation.

## Change
```diff
-    "litellm>=1.74.9,<3",
+    "litellm>=1.74.9,<=1.82.6",
```

## Impact
- Customers using `crewai[litellm]` will be locked to versions we've validated
- No impact on customers not using the litellm extra
- Future litellm updates require an explicit version bump PR
author: iris-clawd

commits.message:
fix: pin litellm upper bound to last tested version (1.82.6)

The litellm optional dependency had a wide upper bound (<3) that allowed
any future litellm release to be installed automatically. This means
breaking changes in new litellm versions could affect customers immediately.

Pins the upper bound to <=1.82.6 (current latest known-good version).
When newer litellm versions are tested and validated, bump this bound
explicitly.

body:
## Summary

Adds native support for 7 OpenAI-compatible providers, eliminating the need for litellm as a fallback for the most commonly used non-core providers.

## Architecture

**One class, data-driven registry.** Instead of duplicating the 2,300-line OpenAI provider for each, a single `OpenAICompatibleCompletion` extends `OpenAICompletion` with a registry of provider configs:

```python
OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_PROVIDERS = {
    "openrouter": ProviderConfig(base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1", ...),
    "deepseek": ProviderConfig(base_url="https://api.deepseek.com/v1", ...),
    ...
}
```

Adding a new OpenAI-compatible provider = adding one dict entry. No new files, no new classes.

## Providers

| Provider | Base URL | API Key Env | Notes |
|----------|----------|-------------|-------|
| openrouter | openrouter.ai/api/v1 | OPENROUTER_API_KEY | HTTP-Referer header auto-added |
| deepseek | api.deepseek.com/v1 | DEEPSEEK_API_KEY | |
| ollama | localhost:11434/v1 | OLLAMA_API_KEY | No key required, OLLAMA_HOST for URL |
| ollama_chat | (alias for ollama) | | |
| hosted_vllm | localhost:8000/v1 | VLLM_API_KEY | No key required |
| cerebras | api.cerebras.ai/v1 | CEREBRAS_API_KEY | |
| dashscope | dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com | DASHSCOPE_API_KEY | Alibaba Qwen models |

## Usage

```python
# Via provider prefix in model string
LLM(model="deepseek/deepseek-chat")
LLM(model="ollama/llama3")
LLM(model="openrouter/anthropic/claude-3-opus")

# Via explicit provider parameter
LLM(model="llama3", provider="ollama")
LLM(model="qwen-turbo", provider="dashscope")
```

## Provider-specific handling
- **OpenRouter**: Auto-adds `HTTP-Referer: https://crewai.com` header (required by their API)
- **Ollama**: Normalizes `OLLAMA_HOST` env var to append `/v1` if missing; no API key required (uses "ollama" as default for SDK compatibility)
- **hosted_vllm**: No API key required (uses "dummy" for SDK compatibility)

## Files
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llms/providers/openai_compatible/completion.py` — ~230 lines (ProviderConfig + registry + subclass)
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llms/providers/openai_compatible/__init__.py` — exports
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llm.py` — routing updates (SUPPORTED_NATIVE_PROVIDERS, provider_mapping, _get_native_provider)
- `lib/crewai/tests/llms/openai_compatible/test_openai_compatible.py` — 35 tests

## Tests
35 new tests covering: provider config validation, registry for all providers, Ollama URL normalization, API key resolution (env/explicit/defaults), base URL resolution, header merging, LLM factory integration.

## Context
Based on analysis of the top providers used in the last 90 days. These 7 providers (plus the 5 existing native providers) cover the vast majority of real-world usage, reducing dependence on litellm for all but the most niche providers.
author: alex-clawd

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Adds first-class support for multiple OpenAI-compatible providers by introducing a single `OpenAICompatibleCompletion` implementation backed by a provider-config registry, and updates `LLM` routing to select it natively when appropriate. **Changes:** - Introduces `OpenAICompatibleCompletion` + `OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_PROVIDERS` registry with provider-specific base URLs, env var keys, default headers, and URL normalization. - Updates `LLM` provider inference/routing to recognize OpenAI-compatible provider prefixes and route to the new native provider class. - Adds a dedicated test suite for OpenAI-compatible providers and adjusts existing LLM tests where LiteLLM is required. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llms/providers/openai_compatible/completion.py` | Adds provider config dataclass/registry and OpenAI-compatible completion subclass. | | `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llms/providers/openai_compatible/__init__.py` | Exposes OpenAI-compatible provider exports. | | `lib/crewai/src/crewai/llm.py` | Extends native provider routing/matching to include OpenAI-compatible providers. | | `lib/crewai/tests/llms/openai_compatible/test_openai_compatible.py` | Adds unit/integration tests for registry, config resolution, and `LLM` factory integration. | | `lib/crewai/tests/llms/openai_compatible/__init__.py` | Marks test package for OpenAI-compatible tests. | | `lib/crewai/tests/test_llm.py` | Updates tests to force LiteLLM path where needed for custom provider parsing/validation. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews.

commits.message:
feat: add native OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Ollama, vLLM, Cerebras, Dashscope)

Add a data-driven OpenAI-compatible provider system that enables
native support for multiple third-party APIs that implement the
OpenAI API specification.

New providers:
- OpenRouter: 500+ models via openrouter.ai
- DeepSeek: deepseek-chat, deepseek-coder, deepseek-reasoner
- Ollama: local models (llama3, mistral, codellama, etc.)
- hosted_vllm: self-hosted vLLM servers
- Cerebras: ultra-fast inference
- Dashscope: Alibaba Qwen models (qwen-turbo, qwen-max, etc.)

Architecture:
- Single OpenAICompatibleCompletion class extends OpenAICompletion
- ProviderConfig dataclass stores per-provider settings
- Registry dict makes adding new providers a single config entry
- Handles provider-specific quirks (OpenRouter headers, Ollama
  base URL normalization, optional API keys)

Usage:
  LLM(model="deepseek/deepseek-chat")
  LLM(model="ollama/llama3")
  LLM(model="openrouter/anthropic/claude-3-opus")
  LLM(model="llama3", provider="ollama")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Tests for _get_custom_llm_provider and _validate_call_params used
openrouter/ model prefix which now routes to native provider.
Added is_litellm=True to force litellm path since these test
litellm-specific internals.

title: refactor: decouple internal plumbing from litellm (token counting, callbacks, feature detection, errors)
body:
## Summary

Decouples crewAI internal infrastructure from litellm. The litellm completion() fallback for niche providers is unchanged — this only removes litellm as a dependency for internal plumbing.

crewAI already has native providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Bedrock, Gemini) using direct SDKs with their own token counting, error handling, and feature detection. But the litellm fallback LLM class still imported litellm internals. This PR makes those work independently.

## Changes (2 files, +118/-62)

### Token Counting (token_counter_callback.py)
- TokenCalcHandler conditionally inherits from litellm CustomLogger when available
- Falls back to plain object base when litellm is not installed
- Same log_success_event() interface either way

### Observability Callbacks (llm.py)
- set_callbacks() and set_env_callbacks() guarded behind LITELLM_AVAILABLE
- Returns early when litellm is not installed
- Native providers use events via base_llm.py, not litellm callbacks

### Feature Detection (llm.py)
- supports_function_calling(): returns True when litellm unavailable
- supports_stop_words(): returns True when litellm unavailable
- _validate_response_format(): skips litellm validation when unavailable
- Native providers already override all of these

### Error Types (llm.py)
- Removed all imports of litellm.exceptions.ContextWindowExceededError
- Uses crewAI native LLMContextLengthExceededError._is_context_limit_error() pattern
- Native providers already use this pattern

## What Did NOT Change
- litellm.completion() / acompletion() fallback calls for niche providers
- Native provider implementations (llms/providers/) — untouched
- All behavior when litellm IS installed — fully backward compatible

## Tests
863 passed, 8 skipped (missing optional deps)
author: alex-clawd

commits.message:
refactor: decouple internal plumbing from litellm (token counting, callbacks, feature detection, errors)

- Token counting: Make TokenCalcHandler standalone class that conditionally
  inherits from litellm.CustomLogger when litellm is available, works as
  plain object when not installed

- Callbacks: Guard set_callbacks() and set_env_callbacks() behind
  LITELLM_AVAILABLE checks - these only affect the litellm fallback path,
  native providers emit events via base_llm.py

- Feature detection: Guard supports_function_calling(), supports_stop_words(),
  and _validate_call_params() behind LITELLM_AVAILABLE checks with sensible
  defaults (True for function calling/stop words since all modern models
  support them)

- Error types: Replace litellm.exceptions.ContextWindowExceededError catches
  with pattern-based detection using LLMContextLengthExceededError._is_context_limit_error()

This decouples crewAI's internal infrastructure from litellm, allowing the
native providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Bedrock, Gemini) to work without
litellm installed. The litellm fallback for niche providers still works when
litellm IS installed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: docs: add guide for using CrewAI without LiteLLM
body:
## Summary

This PR adds a new documentation page at `docs/en/learn/litellm-removal-guide.mdx` that explains how to use CrewAI exclusively with native provider integrations, without depending on LiteLLM.

## Context

The `litellm` package was quarantined on PyPI due to a security/reliability incident. While resolved, many users want guidance on how to reduce their dependency surface. CrewAI already supports five providers natively (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock) — this guide helps users understand and migrate to those native integrations.

## What's included

- **Why remove LiteLLM** — security posture, performance, simpler debugging
- **Native provider overview** — which providers work without LiteLLM and how to install them
- **Detection guide** — how to check if you're currently using LiteLLM (model prefixes, pip check, pyproject.toml)
- **Step-by-step migration** — tabbed examples for each native provider
- **Ollama without LiteLLM** — using OpenAI-compatible mode with `base_url`
- **YAML config migration** — updating agent configs
- **Quick reference table** — common migration paths
- **FAQ** — addressing common concerns

## Navigation

Added the page to the Learn section in all English version blocks in `docs.json`, right after the existing "LLM Connections" page.
author: iris-clawd

commits.message:
docs: add guide for using CrewAI without LiteLLM

Add a comprehensive guide explaining how to use CrewAI with native
provider integrations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Azure, Bedrock)
without depending on LiteLLM.

Covers:
- Why you might want to remove LiteLLM
- Which providers work natively
- How to check if you're using LiteLLM
- Step-by-step migration guide
- Ollama via OpenAI-compatible mode
- FAQ section

title: ci: add PR size and title checks, configure commitizen
body:
## Summary
- Add `pr-size.yml` workflow that auto-labels PRs by size and blocks merging PRs over 500 lines
- Add `pr-title.yml` workflow that enforces conventional commit format on PR titles
- Configure commitizen in `pyproject.toml` with strict schema pattern for conventional commits

## Details
- **PR size**: XS/S/M/L/XL labels applied automatically. XL fails CI. Excludes lock files, CLI templates, JSON, test duration caches, and VCR cassettes from the count.
- **PR title**: Must match `<type>(<scope>): <description>` with allowed types: feat, fix, refactor, perf, test, docs, chore, ci, style, revert. Lowercase description, no trailing period.
- **Commitizen**: Configured with the same type and pattern rules for local pre-commit enforcement.

Both `pr-size` and `pr-title` should be added as required status checks on `main` once they run for the first time.

## Test plan
- [ ] Open a PR with a non-conventional title and verify `pr-title` check fails
- [ ] Open a PR over 500 lines and verify `pr-size` check fails and labels as `size/XL`
- [ ] Verify commitizen rejects non-conforming local commits


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Changes are limited to CI/workflow enforcement and dev tooling; primary risk is unintended friction or non-blocking checks due to misconfigured workflow options (e.g., `continue-on-error`, `fail_if_xl`).
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds two GitHub Actions checks on pull requests: an automated PR size labeler (`size/XS`–`size/XL`) with an ignore list for generated/large artifacts, and a semantic PR title validator enforcing conventional-commit style type/subject rules.
> 
> Updates dev tooling by adding `commitizen` to the dev dependency group and configuring its conventional-commit schema/bump rules in `pyproject.toml`, with corresponding `uv.lock` updates for `commitizen` and its transitive dependencies.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 84fe4f364def8bc4469f5bd9b1b3b65df5299e8d. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
Clean CI improvement. PR size labeler with smart ignores, conventional commit title checks (soft enforcement via continue-on-error), and commitizen config.

Minor:
- Missing newline at EOF in pr-size.yml
- PR title check is soft (continue-on-error: true) — intentional for transition?
- RELEASE_PROCESS.md is referenced but may not exist yet
- refactor → PATCH in bump_map is a judgment call, just be intentional

None blocking. ✅

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues. There are 3 total unresolved issues (including 1 from previous review). Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
Re-approved after latest push. Changes look good: cz_customize plugin, minimal permissions on pr-title workflow, commitizen added as dev dep. ✅

title: fix: raise value error on no file support
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces a new `ValueError` when `files` are provided to models that don’t support multimodal input, which may break existing callers that previously had files silently ignored. Also expands model-name heuristics for vision support, which could affect routing/formatting of inputs for Claude/Bedrock variants.
> 
> **Overview**
> **File attachments now fail fast on unsupported models.** When `crewai_files` is installed but the selected model does not support multimodal, `_process_message_files`/`_aprocess_message_files` now raise a clear `ValueError` if any message includes `files`, instead of silently returning messages unchanged.
> 
> **Expanded vision-model detection.** Updates `supports_multimodal()` heuristics to recognize additional Claude 4 variants (including Bedrock-prefixed forms) so file/image handling is enabled for those models.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 98a8ab8d8c31af110dd7d79c4a3907003c400f3e. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
Clean fail-fast fix — raising ValueError when files are provided to non-multimodal models is the right call vs silent ignore. Claude 4 variant detection expanded consistently across all providers. 

Minor suggestion: add a test for the new ValueError path, and consider extracting the duplicated check into a shared helper. Not blocking. ✅
