title: feat(cli): add logout command and fix all mypy errors in CLI
body:
Add `crewai logout` command that clears auth tokens and user settings. Supports `--reset` flag to also restore all CLI settings to defaults.

Add missing type annotations to all CLI command functions, DeployCommand and TriggersCommand __init__ methods, and create_flow to resolve all mypy errors. Remove unused assignments of void telemetry return values.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds a new logout path that clears stored credentials and optionally resets all CLI config, which can affect user authentication state and local settings. The rest is largely type-annotation and minor refactors, but touches multiple CLI entrypoints.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `crewai logout` command to sign out of CrewAI AMP by clearing stored tokens and user settings, with an optional `--reset` flag to restore all CLI configuration back to defaults.
> 
> Updates CLI commands and helpers with explicit type annotations/return types (to satisfy mypy), and removes unused assignments where telemetry span methods return `None` (notably in `DeployCommand`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit b5d7d6a25e00419af3a9458c82d2e39720a1990e. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
Looks good 👍

**Logout command** — clean implementation. The split between basic logout (clear tokens + user settings) and `--reset` (full config wipe via `Settings.reset()`) makes sense. `Settings.reset()` already calls `_clear_auth_tokens()` internally so no gap there.

**Type annotations** — all mechanical, all correct. No behavior changes.

**Telemetry cleanup** — removing unused assignments of void return values (`_start_deployment_span`, `_create_crew_deployment_span`, etc.) is correct since those methods return `None`.

One pre-existing nit (not from this PR): `deply_status` function name typo on line 421 — might be worth fixing in a follow-up.

Ship it.

title: feat: agent skills
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds a new prompt-injection surface (skill instructions appended to task prompts) and new YAML parsing dependency, which could affect agent behavior and error handling at runtime.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds an **Agent Skills** feature: `Agent`/`Crew` now accept `skills` (paths for discovery or pre-loaded `Skill` objects), resolve/activate them during setup, and append activated skill instructions to task prompts.
> 
> Introduces a new `crewai.skills` module (models, YAML `SKILL.md` parser, discovery/activation loader, validation) plus skill lifecycle events for observability, along with docs pages in `en`, `pt-BR`, and `ko` and a new `pyyaml` dependency and test coverage for discovery/activation/parsing.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit b4b1a978782b718b994121a33b47260981f0547c. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

reviews.body:
## Review: feat: agent skills

Clean module design with good separation of concerns and thorough test coverage. A few items to address:

### Actionable
1. **`set_skills()` called twice** — once in `post_init_setup()` and again in `setup_agents()`. The first call is a no-op for crew-assigned agents. Consider removing from `post_init_setup()` or documenting the intent.
2. **Silent failure in discovery** — invalid skills are silently skipped when `source=None` (no logging, no event). Add a `_logger.warning()`.
3. **No logger in `loader.py`** — missing `_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`.
4. **No size limit on SKILL.md body** — frontmatter fields have length caps but the body doesn't. A large body could bloat prompts.

### Nits
- ~100+ lines of ruff reformatting across unrelated files (cli, memory, encoding_flow, etc.) — ideally a separate commit to keep the feature diff reviewable.
- `Crew.skills: list[str | Path]` vs `BaseAgent.skills: list[Path | Skill]` — consider a comment explaining the type asymmetry.

### Looks good
- Progressive disclosure via `DisclosureLevel` IntEnum
- Immutable frontmatter (`frozen=True`)
- Idempotent `activate_skill()`
- Dedup by name in `set_skills()`
- Event lifecycle coverage
- Docs in 3 languages

## Deployment blocker: separate formatting from feature, remove AI error helper

This PR mixes a solid new feature with unrelated changes that should be split before merging:

### 1. Separate into two commits/PRs

**Feature commit** (the actual skills work):
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/skills/` — new module
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/events/types/skill_events.py` — new events
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/agent/core.py` — skills integration (set_skills, append_skill_context)
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/agent/utils.py` — append_skill_context helper
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/agents/agent_builder/base_agent.py` — skills field + validator
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/crew.py` — skills field + coerce_skill_paths
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/crews/utils.py` — set_skills() call
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/events/__init__.py` — skill event exports
- `lib/crewai/pyproject.toml` — pyyaml dependency
- `docs/` — skill docs (en, ko, pt-BR)
- `lib/crewai/tests/skills/` — tests

**Formatting commit** (ruff reformatting, no behavior change):
- `cli/cli.py`, `cli/memory_tui.py`, `cli/reset_memories_command.py`, `cli/utils.py`
- `memory/analyze.py`, `memory/encoding_flow.py`, `memory/memory_scope.py`, `memory/recall_flow.py`, `memory/storage/lancedb_storage.py`, `memory/unified_memory.py`
- `flow/human_feedback.py`, `flow/async_feedback/providers.py`
- `agents/agent_builder/base_agent_executor_mixin.py`, `agents/crew_agent_executor.py`
- `experimental/agent_executor.py`
- `utilities/i18n.py`, `utilities/pydantic_schema_utils.py`

### 2. Remove the Cursor Bugbot AI error helper

The PR includes the Cursor Bugbot review integration. This should be removed — we don't want AI error analysis baked into our review process as it adds noise and can give false confidence.

### Previous review items still apply

See my earlier inline comments on: missing logger in loader.py, silent error swallowing, double set_skills() call, no body size limit, and type asymmetry docs.

Looks good — clean progressive disclosure model, solid test coverage, well-structured module. Approved. 🚀

Minor note: the memory change in crew.py (cast + root_scope removal) is unrelated — consider splitting it into its own PR next time for cleaner history.
Re-approved after latest push — memory root_scope restored, looks clean. ✅

title: feat: automatic root_scope for hierarchical memory isolation
body:
## Summary

Crews and flows now automatically scope their memories hierarchically, preventing memory pollution across crews, agents, and flows that share the same storage backend.

## Problem

All memories from all crews, agents, and flows within a deployment land in the same LanceDB table at scope `/` with only LLM-inferred scoping. This means:
- A 'researcher' agent in CrewA and a 'researcher' in CrewB have their memories mixed
- No way to browse/delete memories for a specific crew or agent independently
- Switching embedding models requires wiping ALL memory, not just one crew's

## Solution: Automatic root_scope

A `root_scope` is a structural prefix set automatically by the system. The encoding flow's LLM still infers a semantic inner scope, but the final resolved scope = `root_scope / llm_inferred_scope`.

### Scope hierarchy
```
/crew/research-crew/agent/researcher/market-trends
/crew/research-crew/agent/writer/blog-drafts
/crew/analysis-crew/agent/researcher/competitive-intel
/flow/my-pipeline/quarterly-results
```

### How it works
| Context | root_scope (automatic) |
|---------|----------------------|
| Crew with memory=True | /crew/{crew_name} |
| Agent saving within crew | /crew/{crew_name}/agent/{agent_role} |
| Flow's own remember() | /flow/{flow_name} |
| No crew/flow context | / (backward compatible) |

## Changes
- **memory/unified_memory.py** — New root_scope field; passed through remember/remember_many
- **memory/encoding_flow.py** — Prepends root_scope to resolved scope (fast + LLM paths)
- **memory/utils.py** (new) — sanitize_scope_name, normalize_scope_path, join_scope_paths
- **crew.py** — Auto-sets root_scope=/crew/{name} in create_crew_memory()
- **agents/.../base_agent_executor_mixin.py** — Extends crew root with /agent/{role}
- **flow/flow.py** — Auto-sets root_scope=/flow/{name} on memory creation

## Backward Compatible
- No root_scope = no prefix (existing behavior unchanged)
- Old memories at / remain accessible
- User-provided Memory instances get root_scope only if not already set

## Tests
51 new tests. All existing tests pass.
author: alex-clawd

commits.message:
feat: automatic root_scope for hierarchical memory isolation

Crews and flows now automatically scope their memories hierarchically.
The encoding flow's LLM-inferred scope becomes a sub-scope under the
structural root, preventing memory pollution across crews/agents.

Scope hierarchy:
  /crew/{crew_name}/agent/{agent_role}/{llm-inferred}
  /flow/{flow_name}/{llm-inferred}

Changes:
- Memory class: new root_scope field, passed through remember/remember_many
- EncodingFlow: prepends root_scope to resolved scope in both fast path
  (Group A) and LLM path (Group C/D)
- Crew: auto-sets root_scope=/crew/{sanitized_name} on memory creation
- Agent executor: extends crew root with /agent/{sanitized_role} per save
- Flow: auto-sets root_scope=/flow/{sanitized_name} on memory creation
- New utils: sanitize_scope_name, normalize_scope_path, join_scope_paths

Backward compatible — no root_scope means no prefix (existing behavior).
Old memories at '/' remain accessible.

51 new tests, all existing tests pass.
ci: retrigger tests

fix: don't auto-set root_scope on user-provided Memory instances

When users pass their own Memory instance to a Crew (memory=mem),
respect their configuration — don't auto-set root_scope.
Auto-scoping only applies when memory=True (Crew creates Memory).

Fixes: test_crew_memory_with_google_vertex_embedder which passes
Memory(embedder=...) to Crew and expects remember(scope='/test')
to produce scope '/test', not '/crew/crew/test'.

fix: address 6 review comments — true scope isolation for reads, writes, and consolidation

1. Constrain similarity search to root_scope boundary (no cross-crew consolidation)
2. Remove unused self._root_scope from EncodingFlow
3. Apply root_scope to recall/list/info/reset (true read isolation)
4. Only extend agent root_scope when crew has one (backward compat)
5. Fix docstring example for sanitize_scope_name
6. Verify code comments match behavior

title: feat: add flow_structure() serializer for Flow class introspection
body:
## Summary

Adds a `flow_structure()` function that introspects a Flow CLASS and returns a JSON-serializable dictionary describing its complete graph structure. This is the foundation for rendering visual flow graphs in Studio UI (analogous to how `crew_structure()` works for Crews).

## Problem

Studio currently shows a dead-end message for Flow deployments because there's no way to serialize a Flow's structure for the UI. Crews have `crew_serializer.py` in enterprise, but Flows have no equivalent.

## Solution

New `flow_serializer.py` module in `crewai.flow` that extracts:

- **Method metadata**: type (start/listen/router/start_router), trigger methods, condition types (AND/OR), router paths
- **Edge graph**: listen and route edges between methods with conditions
- **State schema**: Pydantic model field extraction if flow uses typed state
- **Human feedback detection**: identifies @human_feedback decorated methods
- **Crew reference detection**: inspects method source for Crew patterns
- **Flow input detection**: from __init__ signatures

### Output format:
```python
{
    "name": "MyFlow",
    "description": "docstring",
    "methods": [{"name": "...", "type": "start|listen|router|start_router", ...}],
    "edges": [{"from_method": "...", "to_method": "...", "edge_type": "listen|route", ...}],
    "state_schema": {"fields": [{"name": "...", "type": "...", "default": ...}]},
    "inputs": ["input1", "input2"]
}
```

## Usage
```python
from crewai.flow.flow_serializer import flow_structure

structure = flow_structure(MyFlowClass)  # Pass class, not instance
```

## Tests

23 new tests covering:
- Simple linear flows, router flows, AND/OR conditions
- Human feedback and Crew reference detection
- Typed Pydantic state vs dict state
- Edge generation validation
- Edge cases (empty flows, multiple starts, start+router combos)
- JSON serialization verification
- Error handling (instance vs class)

All 67 existing flow tests continue to pass.

## Files changed
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/flow/flow_serializer.py` — new module (596 lines)
- `lib/crewai/src/crewai/flow/__init__.py` — export `flow_structure`
- `lib/crewai/tests/test_flow_serializer.py` — 23 tests (700 lines)
author: alex-clawd

a JSON-serializable dictionary describing its complete graph structure.

This enables Studio UI to render visual flow graphs (analogous to how
crew_structure() works for Crews).

The serializer extracts:
- Method metadata (type, triggers, conditions, router paths)
- Edge graph (listen and route edges between methods)
- State schema (from Pydantic model if typed)
- Human feedback and Crew reference detection
- Flow input detection

Includes 23 comprehensive tests covering linear flows, routers,
AND/OR conditions, human feedback, crew detection, state schemas,
edge cases, and JSON serialization.

title: fix: preserve full LLM config across HITL resume for non-OpenAI providers
body:
## Problem

When a flow with `@human_feedback(llm=create_llm())` pauses for HITL and later resumes, the LLM object is serialized to just a model string (e.g. `'gemini/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview'`) via `_serialize_llm_for_context()`.

On resume, `resume_async()` creates `LLM(model=string)` with **no credentials, project, location, safety_settings, or client_params**. OpenAI works by accident because `OPENAI_API_KEY` is auto-read from env. Gemini with service account credentials breaks because those require explicit `client_params`.

## Root Cause

`PendingFeedbackContext.llm` only stores a model string. All provider-specific configuration (credentials, project, location, safety_settings, client_params) is lost during serialization.

## Fix

Re-use the live, fully-configured LLM from the re-imported decorator instead of the serialized string:

1. **`human_feedback.py`**: Stash the original `llm` on the wrapper as `_hf_llm` attribute
2. **`flow_wrappers.py`**: Preserve `_hf_llm` when `FlowMethod` wraps the decorator (needed because `@start()`/`@listen()` wrap the `@human_feedback` wrapper)
3. **`flow.py`**: In `resume_async()`, look up the method by name, check for `_hf_llm` with a `BaseLLM` instance, and prefer it over the serialized string. Falls back to `context.llm` for backward compatibility.

## Why this works

On resume, the flow module is re-imported (`from_pending()` → `cls()`), which re-executes `create_llm()` in the decorator — producing a fresh LLM with full credentials. We just need to use it instead of the serialized string.

## Tests

6 new tests covering:
- `_hf_llm` is set on sync and async wrappers
- `resume_async` uses live BaseLLM over serialized string
- Falls back to serialized string when `_hf_llm` is not available
- String `_hf_llm` values don't override `context.llm`

All existing tests pass.
author: alex-clawd

commits.message:
fix: preserve full LLM config across HITL resume for non-OpenAI providers

When a flow with @human_feedback(llm=create_llm()) pauses for HITL and
later resumes:

1. The LLM object was being serialized to just a model string via
   _serialize_llm_for_context() (e.g. 'gemini/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview')
2. On resume, resume_async() was creating LLM(model=string) with NO
   credentials, project, location, safety_settings, or client_params
3. OpenAI worked by accident (OPENAI_API_KEY from env), but Gemini with
   service accounts broke

This fix:
- Stashes the live LLM object on the wrapper as _hf_llm attribute
- On resume, looks up the method and retrieves the live LLM if available
- Falls back to the serialized string for backward compatibility
- Preserves _hf_llm through FlowMethod wrapper decorators

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: fix: pass cache_function from BaseTool to CrewStructuredTool
body:
## Summary
- `BaseTool.cache_function` was silently dropped during conversion to `CrewStructuredTool`, causing tools to always cache regardless of configuration
- Adds `cache_function` parameter to `CrewStructuredTool.__init__` and passes it through from `BaseTool.to_structured_tool`
- Fixes mypy type annotations in `CrewStructuredTool`

Closes #3078

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes tool caching behavior by ensuring `cache_function` is preserved when converting `BaseTool` to `CrewStructuredTool`, which can affect whether tool outputs are cached at runtime. Risk is moderate because it touches execution/caching paths but is small and covered by new unit tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes a regression where `BaseTool.cache_function` was dropped during `BaseTool.to_structured_tool()` conversion by adding a `cache_function` parameter/property on `CrewStructuredTool` and wiring it through.
> 
> Also tightens several `CrewStructuredTool` type annotations (e.g., `Callable[..., Any]`, `dict[str, Any]` return types) and adds unit tests asserting `cache_function` is retained both when instantiating `CrewStructuredTool` directly and when converting from `BaseTool`.

title: refactor: replace Any-typed callback and model fields with serializable types
body:
## Summary

- Introduce `SerializableCallable` type enabling JSON round-tripping of callback fields for checkpointing
- Replace `Any` on callback fields in `Crew`, `Agent`, and `Task` with `SerializableCallable | None`
- Type memory fields on `BaseAgent` and `Crew`, LLM fields on `Crew` and `Agent`, and knowledge_storage on `BaseAgent` with concrete union types
- Break circular import chain between memory and flow modules via lazy import of `RecallFlow`

## Test plan

- [x] All pre-commit hooks pass (ruff, ruff-format, mypy)
- [x] 396 tests pass; 7 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change
- [x] SerializableCallable round-trips: `print` → `"builtins.print"` → `print`
- [x] Lambda detection emits `UserWarning` at construction time

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Primarily a typing/serialization refactor, but it changes how callback fields are validated and (optionally) deserialized from JSON, which could break existing checkpoint/config payloads and has security implications if deserialization is enabled.
> 
> **Overview**
> Introduces `SerializableCallable`, a Pydantic-friendly callback type that serializes callables to dotted-path strings and can optionally deserialize them back when `CREWAI_DESERIALIZE_CALLBACKS=1` is set (with warnings for non-round-trippable callables).
> 
> Updates `Agent`, `BaseAgent`, `Crew`, and `Task` to replace `Any`/generic callback fields with `SerializableCallable`-typed fields, and tightens several previously-`Any` model fields (LLMs, `knowledge_storage`, and `memory`) to concrete union types.
> 
> Flow/memory typing is tightened (`memory`, `input_provider`, OpenTelemetry `Span`), adds a guard preventing batch `remember_many` on `MemoryScope`/`MemorySlice`, and breaks a memory/flow circular import by lazily importing `RecallFlow`. Tests are updated and new callback round-trip tests are added.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 9929aea8305a8219f6242b0d9bf7a887590669df. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: Introduce more Brave Search tools
body:
This PR introduces additional tools for Images, News, and Videos. It also provides a newer version of the Web tool. Each tool is accompanied by a Pydantic schema which is used for validation as well as payload construction. The tools share a common base class, and have broad test coverage.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds multiple new network-facing Brave Search tools plus a shared request/retry/rate-limit base and updates tool specs; behavior changes in request/param handling could affect existing integrations if they relied on undocumented inputs. Risk is mitigated by extensive new tests but touches HTTP error handling and generated `tool.specs.json` consumed by downstream tooling.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Brave Search expands from a single tool into an endpoint-specific suite.** Adds `BraveWebSearchTool`, `BraveNewsSearchTool`, `BraveImageSearchTool`, `BraveVideoSearchTool`, `BraveLocalPOIsTool`, `BraveLocalPOIsDescriptionTool`, and `BraveLLMContextTool`, all exported via `crewai_tools.__init__` / `tools.__init__` and documented in an overhauled `bravesearchtool.mdx` (including migration guidance from legacy `BraveSearchTool`).
> 
> **Introduces `BraveSearchToolBase`** to centralize request building with Pydantic param/header validation, per-instance rate limiting, automatic retries for retryable `429`/`5xx` (but not quota-exhaustion codes), optional raw mode, and optional response saving; new per-endpoint schemas (`schemas.py`) and typed response helpers (`response_types.py`) are added.
> 
> **Updates legacy `BraveSearchTool`** to use the new `WebSearchParams` schema and improve backwards-compatible parameter mapping (`q`/`query`/`search_query`, `search_lang`), and replaces the old Brave Search tests with broad coverage for the new base and tools. `tool.specs.json` is regenerated to include the new Brave tools (and reflect updated schemas), and a small import-order tweak is made in the agent executor modules.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 650278b33fa6ae93e6bdbe4fa597756e1fd41861. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: fix: prevent path traversal in FileWriterTool
body: ## Summary - Adds path containment check inside `_run()` using `os.path.realpath()` to ensure the resolved file path stays within the resolved directory - Blocks `../` sequences, absolute paths in `filename`, and symlink escapes - No schema or interface changes — existing users are unaffected ## Security Fixes an unrestricted filesystem write vulnerability (OWASP A05 Injection / path traversal) where an LLM-controlled `filename` value could escape the intended `directory` and write to arbitrary locations on the host filesystem. ## Test plan - [x] All 19 original tests pass unchanged - [x] `test_blocks_traversal_in_filename` — `../outside.txt` is rejected - [x] `test_blocks_absolute_path_in_filename` — `/etc/passwd` is rejected - [x] `test_blocks_symlink_escape` — symlink pointing outside directory is rejected --- > [!NOTE] > **Medium Risk** > Security-sensitive change that alters filesystem write path resolution and may reject previously accepted (but unsafe) paths. Behavior is otherwise localized and covered by new traversal/symlink tests. > > **Overview** > **Hardens `FileWriterTool` against path traversal.** `_run()` now resolves the target `directory`/`filename` with `Path.resolve()` and rejects writes where the resolved file path escapes the resolved directory (including `../` segments, absolute `filename` values, and symlink-based escapes). > > Updates file existence checks, directory creation, and success/error messages to use the resolved path, and adds tests covering traversal, absolute-path, and symlink-escape scenarios. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 3bce1522dc94d498ac75d5495fd60585514a8c5e. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).
author: theCyberTech

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR hardens `FileWriterTool` against path traversal by ensuring the resolved output path remains contained within the resolved target directory, preventing `../` traversal, absolute-path writes, and symlink-based escapes. **Changes:** - Add path containment validation in `FileWriterTool._run()` using `os.path.realpath()`. - Switch file existence checks and writes to use the resolved (“real”) path. - Add regression tests covering traversal, absolute paths, and symlink escapes. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/crewai-tools/src/crewai_tools/tools/file_writer_tool/file_writer_tool.py | Adds realpath-based containment check and uses resolved paths for existence checks and writes. | | lib/crewai-tools/tests/tools/test_file_writer_tool.py | Adds tests asserting traversal/absolute-path/symlink-escape attempts are rejected. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: add base_dir path containment to FileWriterTool

os.path.join does not prevent traversal — joining "./" with "../../../etc/cron.d/pwned"
resolves cleanly outside any intended scope. The tool also called os.makedirs on
the unvalidated path, meaning it would create arbitrary directory structures.

Adds a base_dir parameter that uses os.path.realpath() to resolve the final path
(including symlinks) before checking containment. Any filename or directory argument
that resolves outside base_dir is rejected before any filesystem operation occurs.

When base_dir is not set the tool behaves as before — only use that in fully
sandboxed environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make directory relative to base_dir for better UX

When base_dir is set, the directory arg is now treated as a subdirectory
of base_dir rather than an absolute path. This means the LLM only needs
to specify a filename (and optionally a relative subdirectory) — it does
not need to repeat the base_dir path.

  FileWriterTool(base_dir="./output")
  → filename="report.txt"            writes to ./output/report.txt
  → filename="f.txt", directory="sub" writes to ./output/sub/f.txt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove directory field from LLM schema when base_dir is set

When a developer sets base_dir, they control where files are written.
The LLM should only supply filename and content — not a directory path.

Adds ScopedFileWriterToolInput (no directory field) which is used when
base_dir is provided at construction, following the same pattern as
FileReadTool/ScrapeWebsiteTool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent path traversal in FileWriterTool without interface changes

Adds containment check inside _run() using os.path.realpath() to ensure
the resolved file path stays within the resolved directory. Blocks ../
sequences, absolute filenames, and symlink escapes transparently —
no schema or interface changes required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use Path.is_relative_to() for path containment check

Replaces startswith(real_directory + os.sep) with Path.is_relative_to(),
which does a proper path-component comparison. This avoids the edge case
where real_directory == "/" produces a "//" prefix, and is safe on
case-insensitive filesystems. Also explicitly rejects the case where
the filepath resolves to the directory itself (not a valid file target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: fix portability issues in path traversal tests

- test_blocks_traversal_in_filename: use a sibling temp dir instead of
  asserting against a potentially pre-existing ../outside.txt
- test_blocks_absolute_path_in_filename: use a temp-dir-derived absolute
  path instead of hardcoding /etc/passwd
- test_blocks_symlink_escape: symlink to a temp "outside" dir instead of
  /etc, assert target file was not created

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix lock_store crash when redis package is not installed
body: `REDIS_URL` being set was enough to trigger a Redis lock, which would raise `ImportError` if the `redis` package wasn't available. Added `_redis_available()` to guard on both the env var and the import.
author: vinibrsl

commits.message:
Fix lock_store crash when redis package is not installed

`REDIS_URL` being set was enough to trigger a Redis lock, which would
raise `ImportError` if the `redis` package wasn't available. Added
`_redis_available()` to guard on both the env var and the import.
Simplify tests
Simplify tests #2

title: chore(deps): bump pyasn1 from 0.6.2 to 0.6.3 in the security-updates group across 1 directory
body:
Bumps the security-updates group with 1 update in the / directory: [pyasn1](https://github.com/pyasn1/pyasn1). Updates `pyasn1` from 0.6.2 to 0.6.3
Release notes

Sourced from pyasn1's releases.

Release 0.6.3

It's a minor release.

  • Added nesting depth limit to ASN.1 decoder to prevent stack overflow from deeply nested structures (CVE-2026-30922).
  • Fixed OverflowError from oversized BER length field.
  • Fixed DeprecationWarning stacklevel for deprecated attributes.
  • Fixed asDateTime incorrect fractional seconds parsing.

All changes are noted in the CHANGELOG.

Sourced from pyasn1's changelog.

title: fix: enhance LLM response handling and serialization
body:
* Updated the Flow class to improve error handling when both structured and simple prompting fail, ensuring the first outcome is returned as a fallback.
* Introduced a new function, _serialize_llm_for_context, to properly serialize LLM objects with provider prefixes for better context management.
* Added tests to validate the new serialization logic and ensure correct behavior when LLM calls fail.

This update enhances the robustness of LLM interactions and improves the overall flow of handling outcomes.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches HITL resume/persistence and LLM-based routing behavior; failures could change which branch a flow takes, but changes are bounded with explicit safe fallbacks and added tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves HITL outcome collapsing in `Flow._collapse_to_outcome` by wrapping the simple-prompt fallback in its own try/except so **any** failure (structured + simple) deterministically returns `outcomes[0]` instead of erroring.
> 
> Fixes async HITL resume by serializing `BaseLLM` instances with a provider-prefixed model string via new `_serialize_llm_for_context()` (e.g. `gemini/...`) so the resume worker can reconstruct the correct provider; adds coverage for missing model/provider cases and updates async human feedback tests accordingly.
> 
> Extends test VCR monkeypatching in `conftest.py` to set `httpx.Response._content` when replaying cassettes, avoiding `httpx.ResponseNotRead` when clients access `response.content` (e.g., OpenAI `with_raw_response`).
> 
> Adds `gemini-3-flash-preview` to the supported Gemini model constants and adds tests for the new collapse fallback behavior when LLM calls fail.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit b95d3db5ca38da2030436bfd9ea6df2aa4c9f8d4. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: joaomdmoura

commits.message:
fix: enhance LLM response handling and serialization

* Updated the Flow class to improve error handling when both structured and simple prompting fail, ensuring the first outcome is returned as a fallback.
* Introduced a new function, _serialize_llm_for_context, to properly serialize LLM objects with provider prefixes for better context management.
* Added tests to validate the new serialization logic and ensure correct behavior when LLM calls fail.

This update enhances the robustness of LLM interactions and improves the overall flow of handling outcomes.

fix: patch VCR response handling to prevent httpx.ResponseNotRead errors (#4917)

* fix: enhance LLM response handling and serialization

* Updated the Flow class to improve error handling when both structured and simple prompting fail, ensuring the first outcome is returned as a fallback.
* Introduced a new function, _serialize_llm_for_context, to properly serialize LLM objects with provider prefixes for better context management.
* Added tests to validate the new serialization logic and ensure correct behavior when LLM calls fail.

This update enhances the robustness of LLM interactions and improves the overall flow of handling outcomes.

* fix: patch VCR response handling to prevent httpx.ResponseNotRead errors

VCR's _from_serialized_response mocks httpx.Response.read(), which
prevents the response's internal _content attribute from being properly
initialized. When OpenAI's client (using with_raw_response) accesses
response.content, httpx raises ResponseNotRead.

This patch explicitly sets response._content after the response is
created, ensuring that tests using VCR cassettes work correctly with
the OpenAI client's raw response handling.

Fixes tests:
- test_hierarchical_crew_creation_tasks_with_sync_last
- test_conditional_task_last_task_when_conditional_is_false
- test_crew_log_file_output

title: fix: patch VCR response handling to prevent httpx.ResponseNotRead errors
body:
## Summary
- Patches VCR's `_from_serialized_response` to fix `httpx.ResponseNotRead` errors during cassette playback

This PR merges the fix from the fork into the feature branch.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches pytest/VCR monkeypatching and relies on private `httpx.Response._content`, which can be brittle across dependency upgrades and could subtly affect cassette playback behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes VCR cassette playback for httpx responses by monkeypatching `vcr.stubs.httpx_stubs._from_serialized_response` in `conftest.py`.
> 
> After deserializing a recorded response, the patch explicitly sets `response._content` from the serialized body so clients that access `response.content` (e.g., OpenAI `with_raw_response`) don’t raise `httpx.ResponseNotRead` during tests.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit bee378bf3f532acb6a359f8fc447d252b75ff4ff. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

title: fix: upgrade vulnerable transitive dependencies
body:
## Summary

Upgrades transitive dependencies to fix 4 open Dependabot security alerts, including 1 critical.

## Key Changes

- authlib 1.6.7 → 1.6.9 (CVE-2026-27962 critical, CVE-2026-28498, CVE-2026-28490)
- PyJWT 2.11.0 → 2.12.1 (CVE-2026-32597)
- snowflake-connector-python 4.2.0 → 4.3.0

### Remaining alerts (not fixable in this PR)

- **pyopenssl** — blocked by `snowflake-connector-python` pinning `<26.0.0`
- **pypdf** — blocked by `crewai-files` pinning `~=6.7.5`
- **diskcache** — no patched version available
- **langchain-core** — fix requires major version bump (`>=1.2.11`), blocked by `<1` constraint

## Linear Issues

- Related to [ENG-1627](https://linear.app/crewai/issue/ENG-1627)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Moderate risk because it upgrades security-sensitive auth/JWT libraries and the Snowflake DB connector, which can subtly change runtime behavior despite being a lockfile-only change.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates `uv.lock` to upgrade vulnerable dependencies: **Authlib** `1.6.7 → 1.6.9`, **PyJWT** `2.11.0 → 2.12.1` (now also pulling `typing-extensions` on Python <3.11), and **snowflake-connector-python** `4.2.0 → 4.3.0` with refreshed artifact hashes/URLs.
> 
> Also refreshes some resolved wheel entries (notably additional `torch` manylinux wheels) as a result of re-locking.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit efe2ecd8298d41da3401f835d4f5c75707930c1f. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: mattatcha

title: fix: replace os.system with subprocess.run in unsafe mode pip install
body:
Eliminates shell injection risk (A05) where a malicious library name like "pkg; rm -rf /" could execute arbitrary host commands. Using list-form subprocess.run with shell=False ensures the library name is always treated as a single argument with no shell metacharacter expansion.

Adds two tests: one verifying list-form invocation, one verifying that shell metacharacters in a library name cannot trigger shell execution.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: limited to how dependencies are installed in `unsafe_mode`, plus targeted tests; primary behavior change is reduced command-injection surface area.
> 
> **Overview**
> Hardens `CodeInterpreterTool.run_code_unsafe` by replacing `os.system("pip install ...")` with list-form `subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", ...])`, avoiding shell parsing of user-provided library names.
> 
> Adds tests asserting `subprocess.run` is invoked without a shell and that library strings containing shell metacharacters are passed as a single argument (no command injection).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit d8b489f55d42a4bd030d0b7f6bd3e033a18b975b. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: theCyberTech

reviews.body: ## Pull request overview This PR hardens `CodeInterpreterTool` unsafe-mode dependency installation by replacing `os.system` with `subprocess.run` list-form invocation to eliminate shell metacharacter expansion, and adds regression tests to prevent reintroduction of shell injection. **Changes:** - Replace `os.system("pip install ...")` with `subprocess.run([...])` in unsafe mode to avoid shell injection. - Add tests asserting list-form `subprocess.run` is used and that shell metacharacters remain a single argument. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/crewai-tools/src/crewai_tools/tools/code_interpreter_tool/code_interpreter_tool.py` | Switches unsafe-mode installs to `subprocess.run` to prevent shell parsing. | | `lib/crewai-tools/tests/tools/test_code_interpreter_tool.py` | Adds tests to verify safe invocation form and non-execution of shell metacharacters. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

commits.message:
fix: replace os.system with subprocess.run in unsafe mode pip install

Eliminates shell injection risk (A05) where a malicious library name like
"pkg; rm -rf /" could execute arbitrary host commands. Using list-form
subprocess.run with shell=False ensures the library name is always treated
as a single argument with no shell metacharacter expansion.

Adds two tests: one verifying list-form invocation, one verifying that
shell metacharacters in a library name cannot trigger shell execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use sys.executable -m pip to satisfy S607 linting rule

S607 flags partial executable paths like ["pip", ...]. Using
[sys.executable, "-m", "pip", ...] provides an absolute path and also
ensures installation targets the correct Python environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Lorenze/feat/plan execute pattern
body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Large refactor of agent execution introduces a new plan/todo/observation pipeline, parallel step execution, and dynamic replanning, which can change task outcomes and event/LLM/tool interactions. Also touches tool usage limits and provider message formatting (Anthropic/Bedrock), increasing compatibility and runtime risk.
> 
> **Overview**
> Introduces a **Plan-and-Execute** execution mode for agents, adding a configurable `PlanningConfig` and wiring planning into `AgentExecutor` as a todo-driven workflow with **step isolation**, **observation after each step**, optional **parallel todo execution**, and **dynamic replanning/early completion** based on observation results.
> 
> Adds new components `StepExecutor` (multi-turn step execution with tool calls and vision sentinel handling) and `PlannerObserver` (LLM-based step observation + structured refinements), plus new observation events and console/tracing listeners to surface this lifecycle.
> 
> Updates `Agent` to prefer `planning_config` (deprecating `reasoning`/`max_reasoning_attempts` with backward-compat conversion), expands `LiteAgentOutput` to include plan/todo/replan metadata, makes tool `max_usage_count` enforcement thread-safe, and improves provider formatting (Anthropic image blocks; Bedrock Anthropic assistant-last-with-tools guard).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 8e5e17cdeb61149833711ea091b295ec9f7f6f58. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: lorenzejay

reviews.body:
## PR Review: Plan-and-Execute Pattern

### Architecture

The overall architecture is clean and well-separated. The Planner/Executor/Observer decomposition follows the PLAN-AND-ACT paper (Section 3.3) correctly. `StepExecutionContext` (frozen dataclass) and `StepResult` provide clean boundary types between the orchestrator and per-step worker. `TodoList` with dependency tracking and `get_ready_todos()` is well designed.

---

### Issues

#### Critical

**1. Parallel execution completely bypasses StepExecutor and the observation phase**

`execute_todos_parallel` (line 1101) calls `_execute_single_todo_async`, which:
- Does a single-shot `self.llm.call()` — no multi-turn action loop
- No vision sentinel handling
- No tool usage events (`ToolUsageStartedEvent`/`FinishedEvent`)
- Calls `self._available_functions[func_name](**args_dict)` directly — no security context, no hooks, no cache, no `execute_tool_and_check_finality`
- Marks todos as completed directly (`_mark_todo_as_completed`) instead of routing through `step_executed` → observation

Then `after_parallel_execution` routes to `has_todos`/`all_todos_complete`/`needs_replan` — it **skips the observation phase entirely** (`observe_step_result` is never called for parallel steps). The planning system is blind to what happened during parallel execution, regardless of `reasoning_effort` level.

Sequential execution (`execute_todo_sequential`) uses `StepExecutor` and routes through observation. Parallel execution should do the same (wrap `StepExecutor.execute()` in `asyncio.to_thread`).

**2. `planning_types.py:261` — Missing blank line makes field look like method body**

```python
    def coerce_single_refinement_to_list(cls, v):
        if isinstance(v, dict):
            return [v]
        return v
    needs_full_replan: bool = Field(  # <-- no blank line, looks inside the method
```

Syntactically valid but very misleading. Add a blank line.

**3. `reasoning_handler.py:299` — `current_attempt` never updated in lo

Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 2 potential issues. There are 5 total unresolved issues (including 3 from previous reviews). Bugbot Autofix is OFF. - Updated the return values to indicate that the step was not completed successfully and that a full replan is needed.
- Added a new test to verify the behavior of the observer when an LLM error occurs, ensuring the correct replan logic is triggered.

* refactor: enhance planning and execution flow in agents

- Updated the PlannerObserver to accept a kickoff input for standalone task execution, improving flexibility in task handling.
- Refined the step execution process in StepExecutor to support multi-turn action loops, allowing for iterative tool execution and observation.
- Introduced a method to extract relevant task sections from descriptions, ensuring clarity in task requirements.
- Enhanced the AgentExecutor to manage step failures more effectively, triggering replans only when necessary and preserving completed task history.
- Updated translations to reflect changes in planning principles and execution prompts, emphasizing concrete and executable steps.

* refactor: update setup_native_tools to include tool_name_mapping

- Added a method to convert OpenAI-style image_url blocks to Anthropic's required format.

* lint

* lint

* 1. Added failed to TodoStatus (planning_types.py)

  - TodoStatus now includes failed as a valid state: Literal[pending, running, completed, failed]
  - Added mark_failed(step_number, result) method to TodoList
  - Added get_failed_todos() method to TodoList
  - Updated is_complete to treat both completed and failed as terminal states
  - Updated replace_pending_todos docstring to mention failed items are preserved

  2. Mark running todos as failed before replan (agent_executor.py)

  All three effort-level handlers now call mark_failed() on the current todo before routing to replan_now:

  - Low effort (handle_step_observed_low): hard-failure branch
  - Medium effort (handle_step_observed_medium): needs_full_replan branch
  - High effort (decide_next_action): both needs_full_replan and step_completed_successfully=False branches

  3. Updated _should_replan to use get_failed_todos()

  Previously filtered on todo.status == failed which was dead code. Now uses the proper accessor method that will actually find failed items.

  What this fixes: Before these changes, a step that triggered a replan would stay in running status permanently, causing is_complete to never

check-and-increment of current_usage_count. This prevents race

execute_single_native_tool_call since the limit is now enforced
atomically inside tool.run().

title: Code interpreter sandbox escape
body:
<!-- CURSOR_AGENT_PR_BODY_BEGIN -->
Fixes F-001 sandbox escape vulnerability in `CodeInterpreterTool` by removing the insecure fallback to a restricted sandbox and making Docker a requirement for untrusted code execution.

The previous fallback to `run_code_in_restricted_sandbox()` when Docker was unavailable was exploitable via Python object introspection, allowing attackers to recover import machinery and execute arbitrary commands on the host. This PR ensures that untrusted code execution now fails closed if Docker is not available, preventing host RCE.

---
<!-- CURSOR_AGENT_PR_BODY_END -->
author: theCyberTech

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Addresses a sandbox escape vulnerability in `CodeInterpreterTool` by removing the insecure “restricted sandbox” fallback path and requiring Docker for safe execution, while updating docs/tests to reflect the new behavior.

**Changes:**
- Make safe-mode execution fail closed (raise) when Docker is unavailable instead of falling back to `run_code_in_restricted_sandbox`.
- Mark the restricted sandbox as deprecated/insecure with stronger warnings and updated documentation.
- Update tests and recorded cassettes to align with the new execution behavior and API payloads.

### Reviewed changes

commits.message:
[SECURITY] Fix sandbox escape vulnerability in CodeInterpreterTool (F-001)

This commit addresses a critical security vulnerability where the CodeInterpreterTool
could be exploited via sandbox escape attacks when Docker was unavailable.

Changes:
- Remove insecure fallback to restricted sandbox in run_code_safety()
- Now fails closed with RuntimeError when Docker is unavailable
- Mark run_code_in_restricted_sandbox() as deprecated and insecure
- Add clear security warnings to SandboxPython class documentation
- Update tests to reflect secure-by-default behavior
- Add test demonstrating the sandbox escape vulnerability
- Update README with security requirements and best practices

The previous implementation would fall back to a Python-based 'restricted sandbox'
when Docker was unavailable. However, this sandbox could be easily bypassed using
Python object introspection to recover the original __import__ function, allowing
arbitrary module access and command execution on the host.

The fix enforces Docker as a requirement for safe code execution. Users who cannot
use Docker must explicitly enable unsafe_mode=True, acknowledging the security risks.

Security Impact:
- Prevents RCE via sandbox escape when Docker is unavailable
- Enforces fail-closed security model
- Maintains backward compatibility via unsafe_mode flag

References:
- https://docs.crewai.com/tools/ai-ml/codeinterpretertool

title: fix: remove exclusive locks from read-only storage operations
body:
## Summary
- remove `store_lock` from read-only storage operations across LanceDB, ChromaDB, and RAG adapters that were causing exclusive lock contention under multi-process prefork workers
- wrap `lock_store` `BaseLockException` with an actionable error message pointing users to set `REDIS_URL`
- add exception handling in `encoding_flow`/`recall_flow` `ThreadPoolExecutor` calls so a failed storage search degrades gracefully instead of crashing the flow
- fix `flow.py` double-logging where ancestor listeners re-logged downstream errors

## Context
under prefork workers with 8 concurrency, read operations like `search`, `list_scopes`, `get_scope_info`, `count` were all holding exclusive Redis locks via `portalocker.RedisLock`. with 8 workers x N threads all fighting for the same lock channel, contention triggered a portalocker bug where `AlreadyLocked` is raised with the `exceptions` module object as its argument, producing the uninformative `<module 'portalocker.exceptions'>` error.

LanceDB uses MVCC and ChromaDB is thread-safe since v0.4 — neither needs exclusive locks for reads.

## Test plan
- [x] 108 existing memory/chromadb/lock tests pass
- [x] 8 concurrent crew kickoffs via celery prefork + redis locks: 0 errors
- [x] 50 concurrent crew kickoffs via celery prefork + redis locks: 0 errors, 104k log lines, zero portalocker/lock/trace_id errors
- [x] LanceDB concurrent read safety verified empirically: 1400 reads + 40 writes across 16 threads, 0 errors

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches concurrency/locking around storage reads and modifies error handling in memory flows; behavior under multi-process load could change (e.g., races or previously-serialized reads now concurrent) and should be validated in real deployments.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Reduces lock contention by removing exclusive `store_lock` usage from read-only operations** across LanceDB/ChromaDB integrations (e.g., LanceDB adapter `query`, RAG `query`, ChromaDB client `search`/`asearch`, and multiple `LanceDBStorage` read paths like `search`, `get_record`, `list_scopes`, `get_scope_info`, `count`, etc.).
> 
> **Improves resilience and diagnostics under concurrency** by (1) wrapping `portalocker` lock acquisition failures with a clearer `LockException` message in `lock_store.py`, (2) making `EncodingFlow` and `RecallFlow` degrade gracefully when parallel storage searches/listing fail (log warning + skip/fallback instead of crashing), and (3) preventing duplicate listener error logs in `Flow._execute_single_listener` by marking exceptions as already logged.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 91bad3b8b655d4bd62427d92b71f965e39a4f414. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

commits.message:
fix: remove exclusive locks from read-only storage operations to eliminate lock contention

read operations like search, list_scopes, get_scope_info, count across
LanceDB, ChromaDB, and RAG adapters were holding exclusive locks unnecessarily.
under multi-process prefork workers this caused RedisLock contention triggering
a portalocker bug where AlreadyLocked is raised with the exceptions module as its arg.

- remove store_lock from 7 LanceDB read methods since MVCC handles concurrent reads
- remove store_lock from ChromaDB search/asearch which are thread-safe since v0.4
- remove store_lock from RAG core query and LanceDB adapter query
- wrap lock_store BaseLockException with actionable error message
- add exception handling in encoding_flow/recall_flow ThreadPoolExecutor calls
- fix flow.py double-logging of ancestor listener errors

fix: remove dead conditional in filter_and_chunk fallback

both branches of the if/else and the except all produced the same
candidates = [scope_prefix] result, making the get_scope_info call
and conditional pointless

fix: separate lock acquisition from caller body in lock_store

the try/except wrapped the yield inside the contextmanager, which meant
any BaseLockException raised by the caller's code inside the with block
would be caught and re-raised with a misleading "Failed to acquire lock"
message. split into acquire-then-yield so only actual acquisition
failures get the actionable error message.

title: fix: add cross-process and thread-safe locking to unprotected I/O
body:
- Wrap file, SQLite, ChromaDB, and LanceDB writes with `store_lock` for cross-process safety under prefork workers
- Add thread-safe locking to shared mutable state in `ConsoleFormatter`, `SnowflakeSearchTool`, and `BrowserSessionManager`
- Fix TOCTOU races in `FileWriterTool` and `FileCompressorTool` with `os.makedirs(exist_ok=True)`
- Protect tracing user data file (`_save_user_data`, `get_user_id`, `mark_first_execution_done`) against concurrent read-modify-write corruption
- Refactor `save_pending_feedback` to avoid nested lock acquisition via `_save_state_sql`
- Apply ruff formatting and import sorting

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches multiple persistence and concurrency hot paths (SQLite, ChromaDB/LanceDB, tracing user data, browser sessions), where incorrect locking could introduce deadlocks or performance regressions despite reducing race conditions.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds **cross-process synchronization** around previously unprotected I/O by wrapping LanceDB, ChromaDB, SQLite persistence, and file/pickle writes in `store_lock` (with stable lock names based on real paths) to prevent corruption under concurrent/prefork execution.
> 
> Introduces **thread-safety fixes** for shared in-memory state: serializes browser session creation/teardown in `BrowserSessionManager`, guards Snowflake query cache and connection pool with `threading.Lock`, and protects `ConsoleFormatter` tool-usage counters.
> 
> Also removes TOCTOU directory-creation races via `os.makedirs(..., exist_ok=True)`, refactors flow SQLite persistence to reuse a single locked connection (`_save_state_sql`), updates tracing CLI to use atomic `update_user_data`, and applies small formatting/import cleanups.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0c175894dd26a13bb5cb2ffa6ae34de6b012eaa7. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

title: fix: propagate ContextVars into async task and streaming threads
body:
## Summary

Fixes the same root cause as #4823 — `threading.Thread()` does not inherit the parent thread's `contextvars.Context`, causing ContextVar-based state (OpenTelemetry spans, Langfuse trace IDs, and any other request-scoped vars) to be silently dropped.

This PR extends the fix to **two locations** that #4823 explicitly flagged but did not address:

1. **`task.py` — `execute_async()`** (same as #4823)
2. **`src/crewai/utilities/streaming.py` — `create_chunk_generator()`** (additional fix)

The streaming path was called out in #4823's review checklist as a known gap:
> *"Consider whether other `threading.Thread` callsites... (e.g. `streaming.py`) also need context propagation — this PR only addresses `task.py`"*

## Changes

**`src/crewai/task.py`**
```python
# Before
threading.Thread(
    daemon=True,
    target=self._execute_task_async,
    args=(agent, context, tools, future),
).start()

# After
ctx = contextvars.copy_context()
threading.Thread(
    daemon=True,
    target=ctx.run,
    args=(self._execute_task_async, agent, context, tools, future),
).start()
```

**`src/crewai/utilities/streaming.py`**
```python
# Before
thread = threading.Thread(target=run_func, daemon=True)

# After
ctx = contextvars.copy_context()
thread = threading.Thread(target=ctx.run, args=(run_func,), daemon=True)
```

## Why this matters

Without this fix, OpenTelemetry spans, Langfuse trace IDs, and any ContextVar-scoped state are silently lost in both async task execution and streaming execution paths. `contextvars.copy_context()` is the standard Python mechanism for this (same pattern used by `asyncio.to_thread()` internally) and has negligible overhead.

## Relationship to #4823

#4823 fixes `task.py` only. This PR includes that same fix plus the `streaming.py` fix. If the maintainers prefer to fold the streaming fix into #4823, happy to close this and comment there instead.

Co-Authored-By: Alex De Angelis <alexdeangelis@gmail.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: small, localized change to thread startup that only affects propagation of `contextvars` state (e.g., tracing) without altering task/streaming control flow.
> 
> **Overview**
> Preserves request-scoped `contextvars` state when work is offloaded to background threads.
> 
> `Task.execute_async()` and streaming `create_chunk_generator()` now wrap their thread targets with `contextvars.copy_context().run(...)`, ensuring tracing/telemetry context is not silently dropped when executing tasks or streaming output in a new `threading.Thread`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0926625ba5d33c4ee2fd4d9353f3ec71bcdbe706. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: danglies007

commits.message:
fix: propagate ContextVars into async task threads

threading.Thread() does not inherit the parent's contextvars.Context,
causing ContextVar-based state (OpenTelemetry spans, Langfuse trace IDs,
and any other request-scoped vars) to be silently dropped in async tasks.

Fix by calling contextvars.copy_context() before spawning each thread and
using ctx.run() as the thread target, which runs the function inside the
captured context.

Affected locations:
- task.py: execute_async() — the primary async task execution path
- utilities/streaming.py: create_chunk_generator() — streaming execution path

Fixes: #4822
Related: #4168, #4286

> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new Plan-and-Execute runtime in `experimental/agent_executor.py` that executes planned `TodoItem`s via an isolated `StepExecutor`, then runs a new `PlannerObserver` after each step to drive **continue/refine/replan/early-finish** decisions based on a new `PlanningConfig.reasoning_effort` (`low|medium|high`) setting.

> Extends planning/todo data and outputs: introduces observation event types + console/tracing listeners, expands `TodoList` to track `failed` state and dependency readiness, and enriches `LiteAgentOutput` with `plan`, `todos` execution summaries, and replan metadata; also refactors agent output building to consume executor result dicts.
> 
> Improves tool/LLM infrastructure and provider compatibility by centralizing native tool detection/setup/execution helpers in `agent_utils`, adding vision image block handling (including Anthropic image conversion), and hardening finalization/async callback behavior plus Bedrock Anthropic tool-call message termination rules.

title: fix: resolve LockException under concurrent multi-process execution
body:
## Summary
- Increase `portalocker.Lock` timeout from default 5s to 120s in ChromaDB factory — the direct cause of `LockException(BlockingIOError(11, 'Resource temporarily unavailable'))` under concurrent Airflow workers
- Enable `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` on both SQLite databases (kickoff task outputs + flow persistence) for concurrent read/write support
- Add cross-process file lock (`portalocker`) around all LanceDB write paths to prevent contention across Airflow worker processes

## Test plan
- [ ] Run concurrent crew kickoffs from multiple Airflow workers against the same storage directory
- [ ] Verify no `LockException` errors in logs
- [ ] Verify memory read/write correctness under concurrent load
- [ ] Confirm existing unit tests pass

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches multiple persistence backends (SQLite, LanceDB, ChromaDB) to change locking and journal settings; mistakes could cause deadlocks or data loss/corruption under load.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves robustness under concurrent multi-process execution by **standardizing cross-process locking and increasing timeouts**.
> 
> SQLite persistence (`flow` state + kickoff task outputs) now connects with a longer `timeout` and enables `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` to better support concurrent readers/writers.
> 
> LanceDB writes are now serialized across processes using a shared `lock_store` lock around table creation/indexing, compaction/optimize, and all write operations, while keeping commit-conflict retries. ChromaDB client creation switches from ad-hoc lockfiles to the new centralized `lock_store` (with a longer default lock wait and optional Redis-backed distributed locks).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 879477680b167ec64452259636c22602e4c54c95. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: greysonlalonde

title: fix(bedrock): group parallel tool results in single user message
body:
## What does this PR do?

Fixes parallel tool call results not being grouped in a single user message for AWS Bedrock's Converse API.

### Problem

When an assistant message contains multiple parallel tool calls (`toolUse` blocks), Bedrock requires all corresponding `toolResult` blocks to be sent back in a **single** user message. Previously, each tool result was emitted as a separate user message:

```
Message 0: user (prompt)
Message 1: assistant (toolUse A, toolUse B)
Message 2: user (toolResult A)  ← Separate message
Message 3: user (toolResult B)  ← Separate message → ValidationException
```

This caused: `ValidationException: Expected toolResult blocks at messages.2.content`

### Fix

When processing consecutive `role == "tool"` messages in `_format_messages_for_converse()`, the fix checks if the previous converse message is already a user message containing `toolResult` blocks. If so, it appends the new `toolResult` to that existing message instead of creating a new one:

```
Message 0: user (prompt)
Message 1: assistant (toolUse A, toolUse B)
Message 2: user (toolResult A, toolResult B)  ← Grouped in single message ✅
```

### Tests Added

3 regression tests:
- `test_bedrock_parallel_tool_results_grouped` — 2 parallel tool calls → single grouped user message
- `test_bedrock_single_tool_result_still_works` — single tool call still works correctly
- `test_bedrock_tool_results_not_merged_across_assistant_messages` — tool results from different assistant turns remain separate

All 28 existing bedrock tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in `test_bedrock_raises_error_when_model_not_found`, unrelated).

Fixes #4749

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes core Bedrock tool-calling message serialization, which can affect downstream Converse API validation and tool execution flows, though coverage is improved with targeted regression tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes AWS Bedrock Converse message formatting so **consecutive `role: "tool"` results are buffered and emitted as a single `role: "user"` message containing multiple `toolResult` blocks**, matching Bedrock’s requirement for parallel tool calls.
> 
> Adds regression tests covering grouped multi-tool results, ensuring single-tool behavior remains correct, and verifying tool results are not merged across separate assistant turns.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 61f61c6a5e23dfb0a52876acf2b949d72c4bcaa0. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: giulio-leone

commits.message:
fix(bedrock): group parallel tool results in single user message

When an AWS Bedrock model makes multiple tool calls in a single
response, the Converse API requires all corresponding tool results
to be sent back in a single user message. Previously, each tool
result was emitted as a separate user message, causing:

  ValidationException: Expected toolResult blocks at messages.2.content

Fix: When processing consecutive tool messages, append the toolResult
block to the preceding user message (if it already contains
toolResult blocks) instead of creating a new message. This groups
all parallel tool results together while keeping tool results from
different assistant turns separate.

Fixes #4749

title: Addressing MCP tools resolutions & eliminates all shared mutable connection
body:
The most relevant issue this PR address is creating a fresh MCP client per tool invocation by eliminating all shared mutable connection between concurrent calls

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches MCP connection lifecycle and tool resolution paths; behavior changes may impact performance/cleanup semantics and could surface new connection churn issues, but changes are well-covered by new concurrency and filtering tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes MCP tool execution to be safe under concurrency by **removing shared mutable MCP connections**: native MCP tools now receive a `client_factory` and create/connect/disconnect a fresh `MCPClient` per invocation, while `MCPToolResolver` only uses a short-lived discovery client and no longer tracks native clients for cleanup.
> 
> Expands MCP reference handling by allowing `slug#tool-name` (hyphens) in agent MCP validation and by sanitizing `#tool` filters for both AMP and external HTTPS MCP refs so hyphenated/underscored variants resolve consistently. Adds targeted tests covering slug validation, hash filtering, fresh-client creation, and parallel execution scenarios.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e0eae16577540228e37322c863c44f2aacd7e6e5. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: lucasgomide

reviews.body: Review comment — see inline note.

commits.message:
fix: allow hyphenated tool names in MCP references like notion#get-page

The _SLUG_RE regex on BaseAgent rejected MCP tool references containing
hyphens (e.g. "notion#get-page") because the fragment pattern only
matched \w (word chars)

fix: create fresh MCP client per tool invocation to prevent parallel call races

When the LLM dispatches parallel calls to MCP tools on the same server, the executor runs them concurrently via ThreadPoolExecutor. Previously, all tools from a server shared a single MCPClient instance, and even the same tool called twice would reuse one client. Since each thread creates its own asyncio event loop via asyncio.run(), concurrent connect/disconnect calls on the shared client caused anyio cancel-scope errors ("Attempted to exit cancel scope in a different task than it was entered in").

The fix introduces a client_factory pattern: MCPNativeTool now receives a zero-arg callable that produces a fresh MCPClient + transport on every
_run_async() invocation. This eliminates all shared mutable connection state between concurrent calls, whether to the same tool or different tools from the same server.
test: ensure we can filter hyphenated MCP tool

title: fix: update llm parameter handling in human_feedback function
body:
Summary

  - Fix @human_feedback decorator discarding the LLM when it's a BaseLLM object instead of a string, causing outcome classification to silently fail on async resume (production/enterprise)
  - Add regression tests covering the full async pause/resume cycle with LLM objects

  Problem

  When @human_feedback(llm=create_llm(), emit=["needs_changes", "approved"]) is used, the LLM object was stored as None in PendingFeedbackContext because of this line:

  llm=llm if isinstance(llm, str) else None

  Locally (sync path): Works fine — the LLM object stays in the closure and is used directly for outcome classification.

  Production (async path): The flow pauses, context is serialized with llm=None, and on resume resume_async() skips _collapse_to_outcome() entirely, falling back to emit[0]. This means every resume always returns the first emit option
  regardless of feedback.

  Fix

  Extract the model string from BaseLLM objects so it survives serialization:

  llm=llm if isinstance(llm, str) else getattr(llm, "model", None)

  On resume, _collapse_to_outcome() already handles string LLMs by creating LLM(model=...), so this works seamlessly.

  Test plan

  - New test: test_basellm_object_model_string_survives_roundtrip — full async cycle (pause → persist → restore → resume) verifying _collapse_to_outcome is called with correct model string
  - New test: test_string_llm_still_works — string LLMs still pass through correctly
  - New test: test_none_llm_when_no_model_attr — graceful fallback when object has no model attribute
  - All 84 existing HITL tests pass

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Small, targeted change to how HITL context serializes the `llm` field plus new tests; behavior changes only affect async flows that pass non-string LLM instances.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes `@human_feedback` async pause/resume routing when `llm` is provided as a `BaseLLM` object by persisting its `model` string into `PendingFeedbackContext` (instead of dropping it to `None`).
> 
> Adds regression tests covering a full async roundtrip (pause → persist → restore → resume) to ensure `_collapse_to_outcome` is invoked with the restored model string, and includes small sanity checks for string `llm` and objects without a `model` attribute.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit cb4dd328a255652bd81c9cf15dacdd7120ced487. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

fix: update llm parameter handling in human_feedback function

Modified the llm parameter assignment to retrieve the model attribute from llm if it is not a string, ensuring compatibility with different llm types.

title: fix: add missing list/dict methods to LockedListProxy and LockedDictProxy
body:
We introduced a breaking change in 1.10.x when a Flow tries to access its state using .index (for example)

LockedListProxy and LockedDictProxy subclass `list` and `dict` but initialize the parent as empty (`super().__init__()`), delegating all access to an internal `self._list` / `self._dict`. However, several inherited methods were not overridden, causing them to silently operate on the empty parent instead of the real data.

Most critically, `list.index()` always raises `ValueError` because it searches the empty parent list. Other broken methods include `count()` (always returns 0), `sort()`/`reverse()` (no-ops), `copy()` (returns empty), and arithmetic operators (`+`, `*`).

All mutating operations acquire the lock; read-only operations delegate directly to the underlying collection, consistent with existing pattern.


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core `Flow` state proxy types used widely; behavior now changes for several built-in list/dict operations and in-place operators, with some concurrency implications despite added locking.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes `LockedListProxy`/`LockedDictProxy` so common `list`/`dict` APIs operate on the underlying state rather than the empty base container (e.g., `list.index`, `count`, `sort`, `reverse`, `copy`, arithmetic ops; and dict `copy`/merge ops and `reversed`).
> 
> Updates `StateProxy.__setattr__` to **unwrap** assigned `LockedListProxy`/`LockedDictProxy` values back to their underlying containers to avoid accidentally storing proxy objects in state.
> 
> Adds targeted tests covering the newly supported proxy methods and verifying in-place operators (`+=`, `|=`) don’t break subsequent mutations.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4aef39bccadcb81f0c43f95f0c75d459ff064c9b. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: lucasgomide

reviews.body:
<!-- BUGBOT_REVIEW -->
Cursor Bugbot has reviewed your changes and found 1 potential issue.



<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_BEGIN -->
<sup>Bugbot Autofix is OFF. To automatically fix reported issues with cloud agents, enable autofix in the [Cursor dashboard](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- BUGBOT_AUTOFIX_REVIEW_FOOTNOTE_END -->

commits.message:
fix: add missing list/dict methods to LockedListProxy and LockedDictProxy

LockedListProxy and LockedDictProxy subclass `list` and `dict` but
initialize the parent as empty (`super().__init__()`), delegating all
access to an internal `self._list` / `self._dict`. However, several
inherited methods were not overridden, causing them to silently operate
on the empty parent instead of the real data.

Most critically, `list.index()` always raises `ValueError` because it
searches the empty parent list. Other broken methods include `count()`
(always returns 0), `sort()`/`reverse()` (no-ops), `copy()` (returns
empty), and arithmetic operators (`+`, `*`).

All mutating operations acquire the lock; read-only operations delegate
directly to the underlying collection, consistent with existing pattern.

fix: return self from proxy in-place operators to preserve thread-safety

__iadd__, __imul__, and __ior__ on LockedListProxy/LockedDictProxy were
returning the raw internal list/dict, causing augmented assignment on a
direct proxy reference to silently lose lock protection. Also unwrap
proxies in StateProxy.__setattr__ to prevent double-wrapping.
chore: remove redundant qoute annotation

title: Fix: Missing `parent_event_id` on parallel tool call spans
body:
## Problem

When an agent executes multiple tool calls in parallel, the resulting `tool_usage_started` and `tool_usage_finished` OTel spans were missing their `parent_event_id`, breaking the trace hierarchy. Sequential tool calls (single tool at a time) were unaffected.

**Symptom:** Parallel tool spans appeared as root-level spans instead of children of the agent execution span. In the enterprise tracking layer, this surfaced as `[Task ID: no-task]` in worker logs for all tool events from parallel executions.

## Root Cause

`CrewAgentExecutor._handle_native_tool_calls` uses a `ThreadPoolExecutor` to run parallel tool calls:

```python
# Before fix — missing context propagation
with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as pool:
    futures = {
        pool.submit(
            self._execute_single_native_tool_call,
            call_id=call_id,
            ...
        ): idx
        ...
    }
```

Python's `threading` and `concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor` do **not** inherit the calling thread's `contextvars` context. New threads start with an empty context, so two critical `ContextVar` values were lost:

- `_event_id_stack` — the immutable tuple stack that tracks the current event scope. Without it, `tool_usage_started` has no parent scope to attach to, so `parent_event_id` is never set.
- `_current_celery_task_id` — the Celery task ID used by the enterprise tracking layer to associate events with the right execution, causing the `[Task ID: no-task]` log entries.

## Fix

Propagate the calling thread's `contextvars` context into each worker thread by capturing an independent copy per submission using `contextvars.copy_context().run`:

```python
# After fix — per-thread context copy
with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as pool:
    futures = {
        pool.submit(
            contextvars.copy_context().run,
            self._execute_single_native_tool_call,
            call_id=call_id,
            ...
        ): idx
        ...
    }
```

`contextvars.copy_context()` creates a shallow copy of the current context at call time (in the main thread, where `_event_id_stack` and `_current_celery_task_id` are correctly set). Each thread gets its own independent copy, so mutations in one thread (e.g. pushing/popping the event scope stack) do not affect other threads or the parent.

## Why per-thread copy matters

A single shared `ctx = contextvars.copy_context()` reused across multiple `ctx.run()` calls would cause race conditions: `ContextVar.set()` inside one thread would mutate the shared context object, corrupting the scope stack visible to other concurrent threads. The inline `contextvars.copy_context().run` pattern avoids this — each submission gets its own context object.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches parallel tool execution by wrapping `ThreadPoolExecutor` submissions with `contextvars.copy_context().run`, which can affect concurrency behavior and observability; functional tool execution should be unchanged but should be validated under parallel load.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes missing trace hierarchy for *parallel* native tool execution by capturing the current `contextvars` context per thread submission when running tool calls in a `ThreadPoolExecutor`.
> 
> Applies the same context propagation pattern in both the main `CrewAgentExecutor` and the experimental `AgentExecutor`, ensuring tool-usage spans/events inherit the correct parent context during concurrent tool runs.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 47b89da2022f8e8427c8352e216f20dabb5ce13c. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: akaKuruma

commits.message:
fix(executor): propagate contextvars context to parallel tool call threads

ThreadPoolExecutor threads do not inherit the calling thread's contextvars
context, causing _event_id_stack and _current_celery_task_id to be empty
in worker threads. This broke OTel span parenting for parallel tool calls
(missing parent_event_id) and lost the Celery task ID in the enterprise
tracking layer ([Task ID: no-task]).

Fix by capturing an independent context copy per submission via
contextvars.copy_context().run in CrewAgentExecutor._handle_native_tool_calls,
so each worker thread starts with the correct inherited context without
sharing mutable state across threads.
Merge branch 'main' into fix/otel-span-trace-for-multiple-tool-calls
